body:
Thanks a lot. But we do not want that people save data to the `~/.n8n` folder. Only n8n should save data for |"internal" use (like config, nodes, database, binary-data,  ...). That is also the reason why it is a hidden folder (starts with a dot).
If anybody wants to persist data they should use another folder (literally any except `.n8n`).

body:
Hello @DaKyzen , can you please explain how to correctly setup this node or maybe share some exemplary workflow?
Also this isn't n8n trigger node, the name is misleading, you should rename it or, if it possible, change it to trigger node, here some information that can be useful https://docs.n8n.io/nodes/creating-nodes/create-trigger-node.html#prerequisites
author: michael-radency

body:
Hello @michael-radency,

The node is for detecting changes to a MongoDb collection and outputting the change details.

Setting up the node is the same as the current MongoDb node in terms of entering the credentials. As for the parameters, there's only the collection field and the Full Json. The collection field is for specifying what collection the node should be watching, while the full documnet switch specifies whether or not to show only the fields that have changed or all the fields of the record. Hopefully the two screenshots make things clearer.

![Sample workflow](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/73026342/158066224-b7fed701-19f3-4e82-9319-90b79afee38c.PNG)

![Table](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/73026342/158066216-caaad5a4-315b-4d4c-9a44-08cfc8b0200d.PNG)

As for the node not being a trigger node, can you elaborate further? I believe that I followed the instructions on creating a trigger node from your link. There was a bug that I just noticed where my trigger node did not appear under the trigger section in the node list which I fixed. I've also updated the name to be more specific to change streams.
author: DaKyzen

body: Hello  @DaKyzen, thank you for your contribution, I did made some fixes to make it work as a trigger node, also I'm wondering why do you flattening JSON object? for now I did remove that, if this need to be done please consider adding additional option for that

body:
Hi,

This is an initial idea for adding manageable acknowledge for Trigger nodes such as MQTT, RabbitMQ, Kafka and etc. There is room for improvement since it is using `sendResponse` logic from the `Webhook` family nodes.

Maybe this should have its own `sendResponse`? Is it handling properly the `error/shutdown` path?

I would love to hear what could be improved.
author: m2scared

body:
Hi @Joffcom,

If you have time, I would love to hear your thoughts on this one. Thanks.
author: m2scared

body:
Hi @nivb06,

What do you think about this one?
author: m2scared

body:
Hey @m2scared,

I like the idea, I am not sure if "Respond to trigger" is too generic though as it wouldn't do anything for any of the other trigger nodes at the moment (apart from webhooks).

Would it maybe be better as an option in the RabbitMQ node for now if possible? Do you also have an example of when you may use this?
author: Joffcom

body:
> Hey @m2scared,

Hi @Joffcom,
 
> I like the idea, I am not sure if "Respond to trigger" is too generic though as it wouldn't do anything for any of the other trigger nodes at the moment (apart from webhooks).

Understood. I used this name because it can be reused in other "queue" nodes that support NACK such as Kafka.

> Would it maybe be better as an option in the RabbitMQ node for now if possible? Do you also have an example of when you may use this?

I agree. My use case is: the event should be acknowledge only if the workflow succeeds. Currently this is not possible with n8n as far as I know. Is there any other way to archive this?

I will change the patch only for RabbitMQ.
author: m2scared

body: No other way I can think of to handle it at the moment, Makes sense that you would only want to ack a success as well. In the past when implementing a logging system for file transfers with an MQ broker (Websphere I think it was) we would just post a sucess message to a queue / topic and include the original MQ ID as a header but I guess something like that would depend on how the interacting systems are working together.
author: Joffcom

body:
> No other way I can think of to handle it at the moment, Makes sense that you would only want to ack a success as well. In the past when implementing a logging system for file transfers with an MQ broker (Websphere I think it was) we would just post a sucess message to a queue / topic and include the original MQ ID as a header but I guess something like that would depend on how the interacting systems are working together.

I am not quite sure if I understood. Do you have examples? Code?

It seems possible to do since there is a similar behavior with 'webhook' and 'respond to webhool'. My current implementation uses `createDeferredPromise` and works as expected. I don't know if it will work with worker n8n nodes.
author: m2scared

body: Sadly nothing I can share for the old MQ project as it was done using other systems for an old customer, If I get a chance I will see what I can remember but chances are as you are already doing something with MQ it may not be that helpful. It all depends on what your MQ process currently is, So we were getting an MQ message with transfer info in it and would then connect to a server to download or upload a file then anything that happened would be logged to a queue and the MQ ID was passed along the MQ messages. They then had this neat web based system which would show them everything when then entered the ID.
author: Joffcom

body:
Hello @m2scared , thank you for your contribution, as for now it seems that RespondToTrigger node is redundant and too tied to RabbitMQTrigger node, as this node only hold boolean value it probably can be replaced with an option in RabbitMQ trigger node. 

Would be great if you can share workflow with setup where you are using this node, as it will clarify use case and gives  possibility of alternative solution without necessity to add additional node.

RabbitMQTrigger uses amqplib, [here](https://amqp-node.github.io/amqplib/channel_api.html#channel_ack) is documentation that can be useful.

body:
@Joffcom
There is a small distinction between pdf-parse and pdfdataextract in the json structure returned.

| pdf-parse                | pdfdataextract |
|---------------------|----------------|
| numpages               | pages              |
| version                    | -                     |
| numrender              | -                     |
| -                             | fingerprint       |
| -                             | outline             |
| -                             | permissions      |
| text -> string(\n\n)  | text -> string[] |

so if we merge it now, we have a "breaking change".
The question is what is wanted, a "breaking change" or that the structure is adapted to the old version.

```js
{
  numpages : pages,
  version: '2.10.377',
  numrender: text.length,
  fingerprint: fingerprint,
  outline: outline,
  permissions: permissions,
  text: text.join('\n\n')
}
```
author: lublak

body:
Another question: [package-lock.json](https://github.com/n8n-io/n8n/pull/2690/files#diff-053150b640a7ce75eff69d1a22cae7f0f94ad64ce9a855db544dda0929316519).
Should something like this be pushed with?
author: lublak

body:
Hey @lublak,

I think where possible reducing the amount of breaking changes is always worth doing that way it won't come as a surprise to anyone that upgrades without checking the release notes.

With the package-lock.json it is an interesting one and one for @janober to answer.
author: Joffcom

body: @Joffcom i think jear. But also there can be an extra option for "array" result. Without joining it. What about the extra data? like fingerprint, outline and permissions.
author: lublak

body:
Hi @lublak 
While working on reviewing this PR, we have realized that this node is built in a bad way, it is one of the nodes that n8n was launched with, and it didn't get any love since, in fact, it's one of the least popular "core" nodes in n8n, probably for this reason.
Therefore, we don't think that it will be right to make any changes to it and that we should internally find the right time and resources to rebuild the entire node.
One thing that you might be interested in, is that soon we would launch a Community Node Repository, and this node contribution would fit great in there
Would be happy to share with you what is planned for this

author: Joffcom

body: Hey @Joffcom, I was curious to know that my pull request is not yet accepted. So can you please guide me on, what did I exactly miss in this pull request.
author: arjunChouksey

body:
Hey @arjunChouksey,

It is a bit of a tricky one, At the moment we are prioritizing requests that improve / fix issues with existing nodes. Once we are at a better number with those we can then start working through the new nodes , I know there are about 50 new node PRs waiting.

Shouldn't be too much longer, The bit that might lead to this taking a bit longer once we start is the use of that extra package. We tend to prefer using HTTP requests where possible as adds less weight and if there is an issue we don't have to wait as long for it to be resolved.
author: Joffcom

body:
Hey @arjunChouksey,

Thanks for the contribution, We have now created a Community Node Repository which is our preferred method for new node submissions.

Once a node has been published to the Community Node Repository users will be able to install the node from their n8n interface and will be able to use your node quicker, You will also be able to update the node as needed without needing to wait for the review process.

In the future we may reach out to you about including your node in our main release, But for now I am going to close this PR.

You can find more information in the links below, If you have any questions about this or need any help let us know.

body: Thanks a lot! We have to think about if we want to add this. Because by definition should it not be allowed to happen. An array is not allowed directly underneath "json". That said, it currently sadly does happen in at least two known situations (GitHub->Get and on the HTTP Request if an option is not set). We want to fix both in the future by default which would make this option then unnecessary. So we keep you updated.
author: janober

body:
Ahh I wasn't aware of that requirement, it makes sense. So we should expect the HTTP node to `Split into Items` by default if it receives an array.

I'll cancel this PR and leave it to the core team. I'm all good using a function in the interim 👍

body:
Thanks a lot for this PR!

But it looks like it is a breaking change. Because it does not just add a new operation ("list"), it also changes how "Get" returns the data in some cases. I agree that it should be changed but that would probably be something for a v2 of the node, so that it is none breaking. For now, could you please change it so that only a new operation gets added and the behavior of "Get" does not change. Thanks!

- put the change in response data behind an optional boolean `options.returnMetadata`

author: nivbe06

body:
Hmm it looks like my `filter` and `sort` options from my original PR were dropped from the `submission:getAll` operation, this seriously limits the usefulness of the node because I believe it will be extremely rare that users will want to retrieve all submissions in a call (which is why I felt that `query` was a better name than `getAll` - though I understand the objective of consistency) - typical surveys will have 10s of thousands of submissions (ours have millions).

I can submit a new PR to add them back in, but before I invest time into this I'd like to know if there was any specific reasons those were dropped @RicardoE105 ?

Also, I'm wondering about the attachment naming. My PR had logic to name the files after the corresponding question in the survey, which I think will be the most common case (and is guaranteed not to have conflicts). It looks like this was also dropped. This is probably less crucial, but this is certainly less convenient that way... Shall I add this back in as well?

author: michael-dm

body:
Even though adding the parent id solves the issue for almost all cases, I would still like the option to return the blocks as array.
1. There is no "group by key" node so I would need to add a function to do this each time I want to regroup blocks per page.
2. If there are 2 calls to the same page, it would be even harder to regroup the items per call.

So in my case I would continue using the http request node instead since it makes my life easier.
author: knshiro

body: @knshiro I totally see where you are coming from and totally agree that the current solution is not perfect. That is however consistent with how it works with other nodes in n8n. We definitely also have to make possible what you need, but that should not happen on a node-level. Because else we would have to add similar logic to every n8n node. To avoid that, would it be better to find a proper global solution instead that works everywhere.
author: janober
knshiro

body: It's very a valid problem: my preferred way of dealing with it would be to add a 'group by' operation to the 'item lists' node.
author: sirdavidoff

body:
I completely agree with adding the group by feature. I even already gave that feedback because I implemented it for something else.

One last try about why this is case is different:
Even though the endpoint is called get blocks, what it actually is "get page content" and I think we should not get stuck in the semantics but focus on actual use cases. 
So while your flow is to only work one page, treating the content of one page as multiple items can be useful to do things like actions per checklist items for example. If you treat multiple pages you want to get those page contents treated as that.
My use case for example, is to create a basic html rendering for each page I fetch. In that case, the block for me is just a part of the page, it's not an item I want to treat individually (e.g. to produce a `<ul>` I need to be aware of all the `bullet_list_items` in order to group them).

author: d4vidsha

body:
Hi @d4vidsha, niv from n8n's product team here
First of all, thanks for the contribution, highly appreciated
At the moment, a lot of eng efforts are dedicated to improving existing nodes rather than creating new ones
This happens for 2 reasons
1. We still have a lot of work to do improving the existing nodes to provide the best experience for those who already use n8n
2. One of the upcoming features is a community nodes repository, where users can upload their node (and node packages) to npmjs repo, and with a simple UI, load those into their n8n instance. We don't have yet a set in stone release date for this, but it should happen rather soon. One open question is if this functionality would be available for cloud users also. It looks like the answer for this is "yes" but it's not set in stone as well.
Once we would make the final decision on this, and once the feature is released, we could make better estimations to when we could process new nodes contributions (if needed at all)

In the meantime, have you tried using the HTTPRequest node for that? It's not as good an experience as a native node, but it eventually does the work (we also have plans on our short term roadmap of improving the experience on that front)
author: nivbe06

body:
Hey @d4vidsha,

You could pop the docs together if you wanted to, to be honest though while it will make life easier when / if we merge the node it won't move it up the list yet.

We still have a few older PRs to look at before we start to tackle new nodes then it will be handled from oldest to newest with the most requested by the community being handled first.

I have been chatting to Raphael about this one as well.

> @saurabharch what about the other changes?
> 
> I will need to have a chat with the team internally as this includes another package. I like the idea of what it does but I am not sure if it actually solves a problem, Do you see any issues when using n8n with no internet connection at the moment?

I will update the other changes in nodes as soon as possible, yes I saw a when a node sends a fetch request to another remote server API or any third-party service which is not locally addressed it shows falsely show request is sent with status, ok but, this is a cause of using a bull.js in the CLI command for workflow automation which uses Redis as a pub-sub message broker for event-driven functions node function flag are not satisfied with proper error handling an event call.

body:
I believe that's all @RicardoE105 
This adds a "Query Options" collection to the submission:getAll menu:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/59641363/159285271-695ea025-bc0d-46a5-a125-202fd4c9eb47.png)

As well as uses the question name corresponding to each attachment, if no prefix is provided:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/59641363/159287679-d39e9376-80f9-4c2b-a12a-761b3e57a62b.png)
author: Yann-J

body:
Hey @RicardoE105 quick ping on this one, and apologies to pressure you... It should be fairly straightforward to validate since these are small improvements over #2765...

I think the attachment names is maybe less crucial, but the ability to query for form submissions is very much needed to make this node fully useful.
author: Yann-J

body: @Yann-J Sorry for the late response, I missed the first comment. > Hmm it looks like my filter and sort options from my original PR were dropped from the submission:getAll operation, this seriously limits the usefulness of the node because I believe it will be extremely rare that users will want to retrieve all submissions in a call (which is why I felt that query was a better name than getAll - though I understand the objective of consistency) - typical surveys will have 10s of thousands of submissions (ours have millions). If I remember correctly, the filter was not working properly and even if it did, it should have been with the pattern the Notion node has, where you can build the filter either manually with a fixedCollection or provide a JSON. Assuming that is too much work, then you can only add the option to filter with JSON. > Also, I'm wondering about the attachment naming. My PR had logic to name the files after the corresponding question in the survey, which I think will be the most common case (and is guaranteed not to have conflicts). It looks like this was also dropped. This is probably less crucial, but this is certainly less convenient that way... Shall I add this back in as well? Adding this back would not be a breaking change? I believe it will. With the approach that I followed you will not have conflicts either. We use this pattern in other nodes as well.
author: RicardoE105

body:
Thanks for getting back @RicardoE105 .

The filter definitely works (one is applied in the above screenshot for instance... if it wasn't working, that form would return ~300k submissions...) - but crafting the JSON predicate is not necessarily trivial as it's a MongoDB query passed directly to the engine - not very much we can do about this, as this is how the API works. The sample provided in the description, `{"_submission_time":{"$lt":"2021-10-01T01:02:03"}}` is a basic working example.
A `fixedCollection` may not work very well here because the full list of fields is not known in advance, and implementing the full complexity of MongoDB operators might be significant work. I would agree that some very common filters such as submission date and validation status could make sense here, but I don't think that's very urgent...

The current logic is indeed to pass the raw JSON string. Is there anything that you'd expect me to change in my PR?


Concerning the attachment naming, you're right, I realize this would be a breaking change... I think it's not too late to do this, since presumably there are still very few users of this node - and even fewer might depend on attachment namings. I do believe it makes sense to use my proposed logic as the default behavior, since this is what users would likely expect, but I suppose we can also add a new boolean to "Name attachments after their related question". Let me know what you'd suggest here...

The objective here is not to avoid naming conflicts, but to make it easy to identify the correct attachment, since those are always linked to a specific form question (even though the API does not make that link very clear - the value for these form questions will be the attachment's filename). With the current naming logic, the relationship is lost.

For example, one of our forms is used to collect farmer's picture and national ID. These are 2 separate form questions of type `image` with unique names like `picture` and `idcard`. In an n8n workflow, to properly identify these 2 different attachments, using the question name is much more explicit than `attachment_0` and `attachment_1`. I am not even certain if the order is guaranteed in the API's attachments array, and if it is, it would require the user to know the order of the questions in the original form (which cannot be guessed from the submissions API response), and will likely change if the questions are reordered in the form.
author: Yann-J

body:
> The current logic is indeed to pass the raw JSON string. Is there anything that you'd expect me to change in my PR?

It's ok if it's only a JSON string for the moment. Just do it like in the Notion example and only enable the JSON and none option.

> Concerning the attachment naming, you're right, I realize this would be a breaking change... I think it's not too late to do this, since presumably there are still very few users of this node - and even fewer might depend on attachment namings. I do believe it makes sense to use my proposed logic as the default behavior, since this is what users would likely expect, but I suppose we can also add a new boolean to "Name attachments after their related question". Let me know what you'd suggest here...
> 
> The objective here is not to avoid naming conflicts, but to make it easy to identify the correct attachment, since those are always linked to a specific form question (even though the API does not make that link very clear - the value for these form questions will be the attachment's filename). With the current naming logic, the relationship is lost.
> 
> For example, one of our forms is used to collect farmer's picture and national ID. These are 2 separate form questions of type image with unique names like picture and idcard. In an n8n workflow, to properly identify these 2 different attachments, using the question name is much more explicit than attachment_0 and attachment_1. I am not even certain if the order is guaranteed in the API's attachments array, and if it is, it would require the user to know the order of the questions in the original form (which cannot be guessed from the submissions API response), and will likely change if the questions are reordered in the form.

You can add the optional to the PR, so it does not break anything.

OK, both tasks completed @RicardoE105 

See below screenshot showing the updated menu structure, with working filters, as well as a new "Attachments Naming Scheme" dropdown, with a default behaviour replicating the current strategy (i.e. numbered sequence), and a new option to use the original form question.

Default behaviour:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/59641363/161574441-970f0398-2caf-4874-943c-add648c8161f.png)

And with the "form question" option:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/59641363/161574561-908cb3b6-b348-42f8-8d18-1fff9121d651.png)

This also works on the Trigger node:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/59641363/161574656-3d45d7af-9b77-46d8-a0da-76fd7562006c.png)

Can we please have this fairly rapidly 🙏... Without both of these options, none of the workflows we were planning to roll out can be implemented...

body:
Hey @Yann-J,

Just started to look at this one, It looks like when the filter is set to None it doesn't work and throws an error as `filterJson` doesn't exist. I did fix it locally but your repo settings don't allow me to push the change to the branch so if you could fix that part not working the rest of it looks good.

Love the naming feature for binary data I can see why you would prefer that I know I would, The only bit that bugs me you have already talked about with @RicardoE105. It would be great if we could have a simple filter and a json filter this would make it more accessible, I couldn't find any api documentation for KoBo to see if they have a call to get the fields but that would be the place to start. I do agree it isn't urgent but it would be worth thinking about, It could be that we keep an eye on community support requests and if it pops up a lot we can spend some time implementing it.
author: Joffcom

body:
Oops, you're right! I had done my tests with an empty filter, but not with Filter = None in the dropdown...

Thank you, well spotted!

Should be fixed now in the latest commit!

I do agree that building some basic filters with the UI would be nice. I don't believe Kobo will document this API since it seems they're just passing the filter over to MongoDB, so the API is basically the MongoDB one - which is quite exhaustive (and recursive), but in practice we probably only need to support a basic grammar. The problem is the fields, which we won't always know in advance. But for sure this can probably wait, and I'd suggest to get this one released ASAP, as I think the current version (without the filter) has limited usefulness...

Thanks for the review @Joffcom !
author: Yann-J

Hey @Yann-J,

Initial review is complete it has been passed up and is waiting for one last review to make sure I have not missed anything then it will be merged in.
author: Joffcom

body:
@Joffcom @RicardoE105 @janober folks, sorry to insist once again, but I see we missed the last release, and this is getting difficult for us to trust n8n to evolve fast enough for our needs.

My original PR was raised last December. I understand that the initial review took some back and forth to adhere to n8n's standards in terms of menu structure, but when it was merged, 2 important features were unilaterally dropped without discussion, which in my opinion make this node significantly less useful.

This new PR is fairly straightforward and meant to restore these dropped features, which we have now been waiting for for 6 months, as none of our workflows can currently make use of the node as it is now. I've done my very best to make the PR clean and address any comment practically overnight, because my organization is really counting on this node to perform some crucial automations. I hope you can understand my frustration.

Can I please ask for a speedy review completion? 🙏

We had this one on hold while we worked on merging another PR that has come in to update the API used, In the other PR what they have done is made it a bit more dynamic than what I had done based on your original PR.

```
const tokenType = (credentials.apiToken.toString().length > 40) ? 'xc-auth' : 'xc-token';
```

This would allow both options to work in the same credential without breaking anything and I think it might be easier for our users as they can just paste in either option and it will just work.

What do you think?

body:
Hey @M1chaelTran,

I have just been playing with this one, It looks like it adds the value as a string if you were to use `343E343` as an example input. Is that what you would expect?

I would have assumed wanting it to say it isn't a number would be an ideal option, It does show another interesting problem though. If you were to send a number larger than the datatype will allow so `1e+20` instead of sending it as a string anyway like it would do for `343E343` it fails with the previous error.

Not sure if you have any thoughts on this or if this was the goal 🤷🏻‍♂️ let me know what you think.
author: Joffcom

body:
Thank you for looking at this PR @Joffcom.
Because '343E43' is a random ID rather than a number in my case, thus I'm expecting a string.

I would agree with throwing an error, however it may complicate our general Firebase parsing method...

An excellent discovery regards to the integer and float datatype issues.
According to Google Docs, the maximum length is 8 characters.
As a result, the usage of 'e' would be an outlier.
https://firebase.google.com/docs/firestore/storage-size#field-size
author: M1chaelTran

body:
Hey @M1chaelTran,

This is a bit of a tricky one then, So technically this solves your problem but for those who maybe want to use a strict data type for their fields would find that all of a sudden strings would be allowed which could be a problem.

I will have a bit more of a play of a play with it but it seems good.
author: Joffcom

body:
Hey @M1chaelTran,

One more question for you on this one, You mentioned it was a random id and you want to store it as a string... Assuming it is some kind of card number / hex value I guess it could also in theory be 12345678 which would flag as a number and not a string.

Would a better possible solution be to have an option to define the data type so we could implement your change to make sure numbers are correctly handled but also think about an option to specify if something is a string or something else?
author: Joffcom

body:
Hey @M1chaelTran,

What did you think about the above?

what you're suggesting sounds reasonable @Joffcom 
could you maybe demonstrate how that could be implemented?

>  ›   - /Users/ivov/Development/n8n/node_modules/@oclif/command/lib/command.js
>  ›   - /Users/ivov/Development/n8n/node_modules/@oclif/command/lib/index.js
>  ›   - /Users/ivov/Development/n8n/packages/cli/bin/n8n
> ```
> 
> Removing Bamboo HR triggers same error for Cisco Webex and then Elasticsearch.

The issue seems to have been `tiny-glob` not going deeply enough into the directory tree. Switched to `fast-glob` to match `cli`.

> 2. `.d.ts` are still being included, becoming `.d.js` in /dist. The glob in `build.mjs` is not excluding them: `console.log(tsFiles.filter(f => f.endsWith('.d.ts')));`

Fixed. Fast glob supports negation glob.

> 3. The existing TS build process emits CJS with the use strict directive; esbuild is [not doing this](https://github.com/evanw/esbuild/issues/422), but supports a [workaround](https://esbuild.github.io/api/#banner).

Added `'use strict';` via `banner.js`.

> 4. Switching to a non-English locale causes the build to fail:
> 
> ```
> export N8N_DEFAULT_LOCALE=de
> cd packages/nodes-base; npm run build
> ```
> 
> ```
> node:events:368
>       throw er; // Unhandled 'error' event
>       ^
> 
> Error: ENOENT: no such file or directory, open '/Users/ivov/Development/n8n/packages/nodes-base/dist/nodes/headers.js'
> Emitted 'error' event on Domain instance at:
>     at emit (node:internal/process/promises:134:35)
>     at processPromiseRejections (node:internal/process/promises:242:25)
>     at processTicksAndRejections (node:internal/process/task_queues:97:32) {
>   errno: -2,
>   code: 'ENOENT',
>   syscall: 'open',
>   path: '/Users/ivov/Development/n8n/packages/nodes-base/dist/nodes/headers.js'
> }
> ERROR: "build:assets" exited with 1.
> ```
> 
> This issue is absent from `master`. The build stages are now parallel, so I expect the attempt to write to the headers path finishes first, failing to find the dist dir.
> 
> Edit: 4.5. Should we restore type-checking to the build process with [`--noEmit`](https://www.typescriptlang.org/tsconfig/#noEmit)?
> 

Fixed. Ensured folder creation upon build. Extracted cleanup phase to start before parallel execution (duh! 😰).

> ## Minor details
> 5. `'\n[esbuild] Watching for changes...'` is shown both for watch and for build, instead of just for watch. You can use the `watch.onRebuild` arg, refer to the second watch mode example:
> 
> ```js
> require('esbuild').build({
>   entryPoints: ['app.js'],
>   outfile: 'out.js',
>   bundle: true,
>   watch: {
>     onRebuild(error, result) {
>       if (error) console.error('watch build failed:', error)
>       else console.log('watch build succeeded:', result)
>     },
>   },
> }).then(result => {
>   console.log('watching...')
> })
> ```
> 

Added proper build message for build mode and watch mode. 

> 6. Instead of removing the existing watch script reference in `package.json`, we should keep it as `watch:old` just like the old build script. In a few weeks if there are no issues, we can remove both.



> 7. After deletion of `watch.mjs`, `shelljs` is now only used for `rm -rf`. We already have `rimraf`, so `shelljs` might not be needed.

Removed `shelljs`.

> 8. The only JSON files in /nodes-base/nodes and /nodes-base/credentials are codex files `.node.json` and potentially translation files. Both kinds are required cross-package from /cli, i.e. they are not directly imported inside /nodes-base, so I expect that `nodes/**/*.json` and `credentials/translations/**/*.json` should be removable from `tsconfig.json` without import issues.
> 
> ```
> find ./packages/nodes-base -type f -name \*.json ! -name \*.node.json
> ```
> 

Removed irrelevant files from tsconfig.json.

> 9. (Nitpick) Naming of `tsconfig.build.json` - the child only takes care of building /src, but both parent and child are responsible for the entire build, which makes the `.build.` infix for /src potentially confusing. This will become moot once we move src into /cli and skip declaration generation altogether.

Renamed it to `tsconfig.dts.json`. Does this make more sense? How would you name it?

> 10. (Out of scope, tech debt.) We should eventually explore the esbuild minify and tree-shaking flags.

Definitely! I'll create a task for it after this one gets merged.
author: alexgrozav

body:
Thanks for addressing my comments :+1:

1. `.d.ts` are still being included, becoming `.d.js` in `/dist`. Example: `packages/nodes-base/nodes/ActionNetwork/types.d.js` A sourcemap is also being generated for the transpiled type def file: `types.d.js.map`
2. The esbuild dist size is 32.2 MB, the tsc dist was 23.9 MB. Helpers like `__toCommonJS` are repeated in every file in every node. Is this size increase accounted for and tolerable? More info: https://github.com/evanw/esbuild/issues/1984 
3. Typechecking is no longer part of the build process. Is this intentional?
4. Re: naming for `tsconfig.dts.json`, I was thinking of `tsconfig.src.json`, since `/src` is the only dir that it targets.
author: ivov

body:
> Thanks for addressing my comments 👍
> 
> 1. `.d.ts` are still being included, becoming `.d.js` in `/dist`. Example: `packages/nodes-base/nodes/ActionNetwork/types.d.js` A sourcemap is also being generated for the transpiled type def file: `types.d.js.map`

Looks like the `fast-glob` block pattern is not working properly. Filtered using JS.

> 2. The esbuild dist size is 32.2 MB, the tsc dist was 23.9 MB. Helpers like `__toCommonJS` are repeated in every file in every node. Is this size increase accounted for and tolerable? More info: [QUESTION: best way use esbuild to bundle TypeScript? evanw/esbuild#1984](https://github.com/evanw/esbuild/issues/1984)

Not much I can do about this, unfortunately. 

> 3. Typechecking is no longer part of the build process. Is this intentional?

- Added typechecking back when running `npm run build`.

> 4. Re: naming for `tsconfig.dts.json`, I was thinking of `tsconfig.src.json`, since `/src` is the only dir that it targets.


----

- Added `npm run build:fast` which skips type checking and dramatically reduces build time
- I found a way to speed `tsc` up. 
  - Apparently, if you don't refer a project when running it from the command line, you're skipping a lot of boilerplate code. This resulted in a `2s` increase.
  - More than that, if you add `--skipLibCheck`, you shave off another `3s` from the build time at the cost of ignoring `d.ts` file dependencies with errors.

---

~~~
npm run build  16.37s user 1.51s system 235% cpu 7.577 total
~~~

~~~
npm run build:fast  1.35s user 0.82s system 139% cpu 1.561 total
~~~

~~~
npm run build:old  21.49s user 0.99s system 180% cpu 11.919 total
~~~
author: alexgrozav

body:
**Update:**
- Added Turborepo integration with parallel build commands 
- Speed up is now 3x for overall build command (85s to 27s)
- Build caching support via Turborepo
- Parallelized (uncached) dev support via Turborepo
- Moved `NodeVersionedType` to `workflow`
- Removed build:fast script
- Removed tsconfig.dts.json
- Overhead is now `nodes-base` and `editor-ui`, with longest running build command, but parallelization helps a lot

body:
Export tests
* Single-file export via CLI: `packages/cli/bin/n8n export:workflow --all --input=wf.json --pretty` → Pass: JSON contains correct tags
* Multi-file export via CLI: `packages/cli/bin/n8n export:workflow --all --separate --output=mydir --pretty` → Pass: JSON contains correct tags
* Single-workflow export via UI → Pass: JSON contains correct tags

Import tests
* Single-file import via CLI: `packages/cli/bin/n8n import:workflow --input=wfs.json` → Fail: Single file containing two workflows, each workflow referencing the same tag, triggers `SQLITE_CONSTRAINT: UNIQUE constraint failed: tag_entity.name`. Example: `[{ "id": 1, ..., "tags": [ { "name": "tagX", ... } ] }, { "id": 2, ..., "tags": [ { "name": "tagX", ... } ] }]`
* Multi-file import via CLI: `packages/cli/bin/n8n import:workflow --separate --input=mydir` → Fail: Multiple files, each containing a workflow referencing the same tag, triggers `SQLITE_CONSTRAINT: UNIQUE constraint failed: tag_entity.name`. Example: `{ "id": 1, ..., "tags": [ { "name": "tagX", ... } ] }` and `{ "id": 2, ..., "tags": [ { "name": "tagX", ... } ] }`
* Single-workflow import via UI → Pass: Displayed workflow is correctly tagged. (But imported workflow is not named per JSON - unclear if it should be.)
author: ivov

body:
## Import tests

Single-file import via CLI: `packages/cli/bin/n8n import:workflow --input=wfs.json` with two workflows having a shared tag
- Pass on sqlite
- Fail on mysql: `An error occurred while importing workflows. See log messages for details. Duplicate entry 'tagX' for key 'tag_entity.IDX_8f949d7a3a984759044054e89b'`
- Fail on postgres: `An error occurred while importing workflows. See log messages for details. duplicate key value violates unique constraint "idx_812eb05f7451ca757fb98444ce"`
 
Multi-file import via CLI: `packages/cli/bin/n8n import:workflow --separate --input=mydir` with two workflows having a shared tag
- Pass on sqlite
- Fail on mysql: `An error occurred while importing workflows. See log messages for details. Duplicate entry 'tagX' for key 'tag_entity.IDX_8f949d7a3a984759044054e89b'`
- Fail on postgres: `An error occurred while importing workflows. See log messages for details. duplicate key value violates unique constraint "idx_812eb05f7451ca757fb98444ce"`

Single-workflow import via UI from file
- Pass on sqlite, mysql, postgres: Tags saved correctly, but workflow name not set from JSON. (Not clear if workflow name not set from JSON is intended behavior.)

Single-workflow import via UI from URL
- Did not test, same logic as importing from file import.

## Export tests

All-workflows export via CLI: `packages/cli/bin/n8n export:workflow --all --output=all.json --pretty`
- Pass on sqlite: JSON contains correct tags
- Pass on mysql: JSON contains correct tags
- Pass on postgres: JSON contains correct tags

Single-workflow export via CLI: `packages/cli/bin/n8n export:workflow --id=1 --output=1.json --pretty`
- Pass on sqlite: JSON contains correct tags
- Pass on mysql: JSON contains correct tags
- Pass on postgres: JSON contains correct tags

body:
@Joffcom 

Replying to a message [here](https://community.n8n.io/t/oauth-authorization-error-zendesk-oauth2-api-credential/12439/29?u=hasanhw):
> It looks like it was reviewed internally but the PR only covers one endpoint for one case rather than removing the requirement fully for authentication on that end point

Yes the reason behind my solution is the following:
- `getCredentialForUser` which is also using `whereClause`
- both requires a User injected to the request
- My hunch is there is a middleware somewhere doing this automatically if the request URL requires authentication
- Since the url was whitelisted, User was not attached to the request

I didn't find where the user is being injected to I went with this approach

author: zebreus

body:
Hello @Zebreus I made some adjustment to batch get operation so it will behave in more expected way regarding continueOnFail, also I think we need to remove error parsing from continueOnFail as it can cause errors by itself and just add it to return data, as it generally done in other nodes. Please let me know if you have any suggestion about it. 

Delete operations always returns success true, even if it was not successful.

Upsert operation is not working as expected, for example here I would expect that it creates a new object
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/88898367/167621253-f92d26f0-f92a-46c9-b5fa-31bf42b7076d.png)
author: michael-radency

body:
Hey @michael-radency It was probably the best choice to remove the complicated error parsing for the batch operations.

Do you have an example for when the delete operatioon returns true, when it should not? I think the only case a delete operation should error is when the resource exists, but cannot be deleted.

Upserting only works with a custom ID, because we cannot choose the HubSpot ID of the newly created object. A good fix would probably be to disable upserting by the HubSpot object ID. I will add that later.
author: zebreus

body: Hello @Zebreus , issue with delete that we do not know if operation was successful, it just returns same value when it deleted object or this object don't exist, and as there are issues with upserting by hubspot ID we should probably disable it. Please let me know when it is ready for review.
author: michael-radency

body: I think the behaviour on delete is somewhat expected. A delete operation is successful, if the element does not exist afterwards. This is also the way the HubSpot API does deletion (i.E. https://api.hubapi.com/crm/v3/objects/contact/834872398 returns `204` and not `404` , even if there is no contact with the id 834872398).
author: zebreus

body: @michael-radency This PR is now ready for review again. I disabled upserting with the HubSpot object ID. I also added some resources for Associations, Properties and Property Groups I had already written. Originally it was planned to add these in a separate PR, but I won't be able to spent much time with HubSpot in the future, so it is probably best to include them here.

body: @michael-radency Yeah, amqplib doesn't do anything on errors but fire events. You'd have to listen for the right error events, and then recreate the connection, consumer, queues, etc, which is what the connection manager does. There's also supposed to be only 1 connection with many consumers, but that's not how the old node was working.

author: agobrech

body:
@agobrech
The reconnect and the parallel executions look good. It seems like there should be a default prefetch, but as long as we configure one it's fine. (While testing it, starting a workflow with just the trigger, the default options, and 100 messages in a queue completely overloaded n8n using up 10+ GiB of ram and 8 cores.)

There are still a few other things from that pr that are missing though. I added the ability to subscribe to an exchange or topic. What's in there now was pretty confusing at first because you can send messages into an exchange, and the trigger says, "queue / exchange" but it only actually works with a queue. The only way to get messages from a topic is to manually create the queue and binding outside of n8n, or by using an HTTP request node attached to a trigger for workflow activated.

The pr also adds the ability to set separate options on messages besides headers, like priority, or content type, which can use expressions so they're different per item. (The trigger looked at the content type header and if it was json, it would parse it without needing the option set.) That might be a part of #2806 but with RabbitMQ a TTL can be set as an argument on the queue, and/or on specific messages.

The current node does open and close the connection each time it publishes messages, which goes against [RabbitMQ's best practices](https://www.cloudamqp.com/blog/part4-rabbitmq-13-common-errors.html). I think I fixed in that pr, but I'm not sure how much of a performance improvement that really has.

body:
Hey @Ansarissab,

Thanks for the contribution, We have now created a Community Node Repository which is our preferred method for new node submissions.

Once a node has been published to the Community Node Repository users will be able to install the node from their n8n interface and will be able to use your node quicker, You will also be able to update the node as needed without needing to wait for the review process.

In the future we may reach out to you about including your node in our main release, But for now I am going to close this PR.

You can find more information in the links below, If you have any questions about this or need any help let us know.

Building Community Nodes: https://docs.n8n.io/integrations/community-nodes/build-community-nodes/
Submitting Community Nodes: https://docs.n8n.io/integrations/creating-nodes/deploy/submit-community-nodes/
Published Nodes: https://www.npmjs.com/search?q=keywords:n8n-community-node-package

body:
Hi @michael-radency, @nivb06, @Joffcom,

This PR references: https://github.com/n8n-io/n8n/pull/2646

My goal with this implementation is to allow one to control if a message will be acknowledged and removed from the queue or if it will be not acknowledged and put back to the queue. This can be reused in other message queue systems in the future.

Example:

```
Trigger -> if (Math.round(Math.random()) == 1) -> Ack
           else                                -> Nack
```

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/31630/167521576-87cf1c29-aa11-472c-a78d-4ef11ff39890.png)

I am still not very confident with this solution. It does not work with `manualTriggerFunction` because the `channel` is closed before the message is `acked`. `RabbitMQ` requires a message to be acked or nacked at the same channel that it was pulled.  At least it works fine with background processes.

Do you have any suggestions?
author: tinti

body: Thanks a lot for your contribution but I am closing this PR as the same logic got in the meantime already implemented but a little bit different to not require an additional response node. If we want to add it via a special node in the future we can use this PR.

body: https://github.com/n8n-io/n8n/pull/3318/commits/f1a747f7e08c34cb5e64dd3205e8f68c2470762d reintroduces a duplication of code, but it's better that it's in the `if scan` statement, so I've removed the latter lines.
author: ctrl-freak

body: @ctrl-freak @michael-radency had a look at the PR. Why did we remove the FilterExpression parameter when querying (not scanning). What am I missing? This looks to me like a breaking change. That parameter can be sent when querying as well. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/amazondynamodb/latest/APIReference/API_Query.html#DDB-Query-request-FilterExpression
author: RicardoE105

body:
The FilterExpression parameter is still in the request; before https://github.com/n8n-io/n8n/pull/3318/commits/d0f0e8dbad2a59c3d4c3d16074b1d304df95029a the FilterExpression value was retrieved via `getNodeParameter` twice on :312 and :324

My commit took it out of the if statement on :311 because there is a later truthy check on :341.

Only looking at the diff and not expanding the lines makes it look like I'm removing the property entirely, but because of the duplication it's still there. I guess which is why @michael-radency restored it with https://github.com/n8n-io/n8n/pull/3318/commits/f1a747f7e08c34cb5e64dd3205e8f68c2470762d

It's probably best that it is retrieved in the `if scan` statement, as restored by @michael-radency, just in case it's somehow set still on the node while scan is false. So I removed the latter `getNodeParameter` with https://github.com/n8n-io/n8n/pull/3318/commits/86e33fcf43ad5658bf76a4f974af29383c90840a.

Hope this clarifies.
author: ctrl-freak

body:
> The FilterExpression parameter is still in the request; before [d0f0e8d](https://github.com/n8n-io/n8n/commit/d0f0e8dbad2a59c3d4c3d16074b1d304df95029a) the FilterExpression value was retrieved via `getNodeParameter` twice on :312 and :324

Hi @ctrl-freak , that are different filterExpressions one is for field when scan is true, other one for option in Options when scan is false, I did restore code related to that

> Hi @ctrl-freak , that are different filterExpressions one is for field when scan is true, other one for option in Options when scan is false, I did restore code related to that

body: There may be some confusion of the `Remaining Estimate` and the delta, or aggregate estimate, `Σ Remaining Estimate`. Here's the JSON snippet returned from the API when you add `names` to the expand parameter: ```json "names": { [..] "timeestimate": "Remaining Estimate", [..] "aggregatetimeestimate": "Σ Remaining Estimate", }, ``` I was mainly focussing on datetime and rich text fields, however I have noticed that the Jira API either omits fields, or returns nulls for example: With that I've used mergeWith from lodash that will use the `.fields` value if the `.renderedFields` value is null (https://github.com/n8n-io/n8n/pull/3323/commits/56e49054d04f223d36f2bf2fcacca28381b91d03). Now the diff between the simplified renderedFields, and simplified non-rendered fields went from this: to this: Much more consistent!
author: pemontto

body:

Heading 2 test *Description* {color:#ff5630}*Some bold orange text*{color}...` | `

Heading 1

Heading 2

test Description Some bold orange text

` | | `Due date` | `2022-10-06` |`06/Oct/22` | | `Σ Remaining Estimate` | `5400` | `1 hour, 30 minutes` |

Assuming this will be configurable by the node developer?
author: pemontto

body:
> 🙌 this will solve so many head scratching moments for new users! E.g. in the Jira node, choosing the `Server` vs `Cloud` option **before** the auth dropdown. Same thing with all the Oauth2 vs API key nodes 🎉.
> 
> Assuming this will be configurable by the node developer?

I don't think it's currently configurable on the node level, but that's really good suggestion. Thanks, hopefully we'll be able to work on in in soon.
author: MiloradFilipovic

body:
@MiloradFilipovic Great work on this! I have a few remarks regarding the implementation of it, going forward:
- The configuration of the credentials index will be done by the node developer, not by the user, as @pemontto said, therefore the `nodeCredentialsIndex` field will be an internal field, defined only inside the `packages/nodes-base` node definitions.
- The index configuration field will not be visible anywhere within the UI itself, it's for internal use only
- The `nodeCredentialsIndex` will be available directly via the node configuration in the frontend (no need for a prop, should already be there)
- If the `nodeCredentialsIndex` field is missing, default to `0`.

author: voko3456

body:
> hey @Joffcom @janober 🙂
> 
> the base structure of the node is ready and working. for now, it can send text, media, and basic templated messages (although Wapp's API is buggy as mentioned in the description). There are 3 things still missing that I will keep working on:
> 
> 1. trigger node
> 2. template components: template messages can have what the [spec](https://developers.facebook.com/docs/whatsapp/cloud-api/reference/messages#template-object) refers to as components, which can be passed to dynamic templates (`hello {{1}}`)  by adding the following parameters to the template property:
> 
> ```json
> "components": [
>     {
>         "type": "body",
>         "parameters": 
>         [
>             {
>                 "text": "world",
>                 "type": "text"
>             }
>         ]
>     }
> ]
> ```
> 
> I am trying to figure out what would be the best way to include this in n8n. what I thought is doing something like in the switch node, in this case, you would add x amount of components and parameterize them. I will keep on working on this one and lmk if you have any suggestions 3. media management: use WhatsApp's API to manage business' media

Maybe you can use set to handle parameters

author: codecov

body:
Hey @Yann-J,

What would happen if someone was using the elevation and precision values? Would it be worth having a bool as an option to disable those values so it defaults to true and doesn't break anything for current users?
author: Joffcom

body:
Hey @Joffcom actually these values were never working because they're not actually supported by the GeoJSON format (the formatting worked but the downstream service probably never understood it)...
For now it's just one global "reformat?" option, but I agree at some point we might need to have different toggles for the different reformatting steps.
author: Yann-J

body:
Hey @Yann-J,

That is perfect, if there was never any value returned then we won't need to worry. I will pass this one on to the next step.

We are currently not very deep in user handling :sweat_smile:

author: pemontto

body:
Findings:
1. If the user toggles on `JSON`, inserts data and clicks `Save`, the output pane toggles back to `Table`.
2. When pushing the center pane to the right, the last word in the footer overflows.
3. When pushing the center pane to the right until the footer text grows to three lines, the spacing between pindata editor and top buttons is lost.
4. The `JSON/Table` toggle seems to have a bit more bottom padding than top padding. According to the design it should be centered in its container.
5. Pin button should not be showing on input pane.
6. When the pindata editor is open and the user clicks on `Execute node`, I would expect the pindata editor to discard changes and autoclose. Maybe a question for Product.
7. The pindata editor seems to be misvalidating JSON, e.g. `{ hello: true }` triggers `; expected`
author: ivov

body:
> Findings:
> 
> 1. If the user toggles on `JSON`, inserts data and clicks `Save`, the output pane toggles back to `Table`.
> 2. When pushing the center pane to the right, the last word in the footer overflows.
> 3. When pushing the center pane to the right until the footer text grows to three lines, the spacing between pindata editor and top buttons is lost.
> 4. The `JSON/Table` toggle seems to have a bit more bottom padding than top padding. According to the design it should be centered in its container.
> 5. Pin button should not be showing on input pane.
> 6. When the pindata editor is open and the user clicks on `Execute node`, I would expect the pindata editor to discard changes and autoclose. Maybe a question for Product.
> 7. The pindata editor seems to be misvalidating JSON, e.g. `{ hello: true }` triggers `; expected`

1. Fixed. Also, JSON / Table toggle is no longer visible while editing
2. I suppose this is intended. Should we have a minimum width? 
3. There's something weird going on with Monaco's resize listener. It seems to be lagging behind for some reason. Need to investigate.
4. Fixed in master.
5. Done.
6. Added prompt. Done.
7. Validation working now! :)
author: alexgrozav

body:
Thanks for addressing everything!

2. From what I remember, the last word overflowed pretty badly, so it cannot have been intended, unless other changes since then made it not look too bad now. In any case, if needed truncation would be nice. Is rather an edge case.

body:
Sorry for butting in... I have been actually testing this node on my workflows and I found something that is missing.

On Template Components -> Parameters

Only "Text" is available as a parameter, but for headers of the template messages you can use image, video and document objects also.

See https://developers.facebook.com/docs/whatsapp/cloud-api/reference/messages#interactive-object (Header object)
Example of the template message with the image header: https://developers.facebook.com/docs/whatsapp/cloud-api/guides/send-message-templates#media-based

Edit: Also noticed this error with Button templates:

"description": "button: Missing parameter sub_type for button component"

Once a node has been published to the Community Node Repository users will be able to install the node from their n8n interface and will be able to use your node quicker, You will also be able to update the node as needed without needing to wait for the review process.

author: Joffcom

body:
@krynble Thanks for reviewing this.

> changing the third argument to become mandatory 

This is not a reliable check because 
- some nodes have no item index, e.g. trigger nodes
- `loadOptions` methods have no item index
- some errors are thrown outside the node loop, e.g. Gitlab
- most helpers in `GenericFunctions` have no access to the index without refactoring

That said, I am expecting true undetected cases (item index available but unused) to come up here and there. Most of the value from this PR is auto-adding indexes to the majority of errors.

author: Joffcom

body:
Hi n8n,

This PR implements the following feature:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/31630/178454147-1c2f900d-aba3-4469-9d35-f98024a617ca.png)

It uses the Redis INCR documentation (see: https://redis.io/commands/incr/). The only uncomfortable aspect for me is that the `ip` array can get very large. What do you think? Should I switch to a simple `MULTI([ INCR , EXPIRE ])`?

```
FUNCTION LIMIT_API_CALL(ip)
current = LLEN(ip)
IF current > 10 THEN
    ERROR "too many requests per second"
ELSE
    IF EXISTS(ip) == FALSE
        MULTI
            RPUSH(ip,ip)
            EXPIRE(ip,1)
        EXEC
    ELSE
        RPUSHX(ip,ip)
    END
    PERFORM_API_CALL()
END
```

body:
Thanks!

Package by package:
- /cli, /core, /node-dev, /workflow: Removed `tslint` dependency
- /design-system, /editor-ui, /nodes-base: Kept `tslint` because still in use

Where `tslint` is still in use, we first need to reconcile those rules with our `.eslintrc.js`, remove `tslint` dep, remove `tslint` exceptions, remove `tslint` from the commands, run ESLint, add its exceptions. This additional work is [tracked separately](https://linear.app/n8n/issue/N8N-4119/phase-out-tslint).

body: > Thanks for taking this on! > > 1. If we pin a node, connect the next, reference from next to previous using expression, the expression works. If then we add a third node standalone (unconnected), run this third node, and go back to inspect the expression, it now says `[invalid (workflowRunData[parentNode[0]] is undefined)]` > > ![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/44588767/179946666-d0a91bce-f157-41a7-b63a-8a5bcffdb373.png) Good catch! Found it as well after more thorough testing. Added a fix for it. > 2. If we pin a node, connect the next, run the next, when opening the NDV or the expression edit modal, there are Vue store state mutation warnings and watcher callback error warnings in the console. These fire multiple times when selecting any option (pindata-based or not) from the variable selector. > > Updated. Rebuilding workflow run data using spreading to be able to manipulate it.

author: janober

body:
Hey guys, just letting you know that this release might cause several issues to a lot of people that rely on N8N and update the N8N NPM package automatically after new minor releases.

The "0.193.0" release seems to be a release with breaking changes, but Node is not able to identify it as a breaking change given the version convention N8N uses for its releases.

A lot of workflows will be broken because of the addition of the '{pairedItem: x}' object inside responses from nodes. 

One example: Before this release, the response from Webflow nodes would give a JSON that would have the data from webflow, such as `{id:"", name:"", created_at:"",...}`. Now, the response from Webflow nodes consists of something like this: `{json: { id:"", name: "", created_at: "",...}, pairedItem: 2}`

This breaks automations that would use expressions like `$json['id']`, because they must update things and use `$json['json']['id']` instead.
author: Jeandcc

body:
Hey @Jeandcc thank you for reporting this.

There is indeed an issue with some of the nodes - the changes we did might cause them to break.

I'll be checking this now since this is not the intended behavior.

author: netroy

body:
Have you seen [this](https://github.com/vercel/turborepo/issues/839)? Can you help me understand how we expect the `incremental` flag to interact with Turborepo cache artifacts?

Having tested making a single change and rebuilding with and without this flag, I am not seeing any noticeable speed improvement:

- TR only, cache hit: 2.265s
- TR + flag, cache hit: 2.124s
- TR only, cache miss: 19.916s
- TR + flag, cache miss: 19.928s

```
Model Name: MacBook Pro
Model Identifier: MacBookPro17,1
Chip: Apple M1
Total Number of Cores: 8 (4 performance and 4 efficiency)
Memory: 16 GB
```
author: ivov

body:
@ivov i hadn't seen that issue. I'll check it now.

Can you please delete your `tsc` cache (`rm -rf packages/**/tsconfig.tsbuildinfo`) and run the benchmark once more?
author: netroy

body: @netroy Could you please provide exact steps to be able to see the difference? The difference I'm seeing is negligible when building on top of `tsconfig.tsbuildinfo` vs. when using `rm -rf packages/**/tsconfig.tsbuildinfo`and then building, while ensuring that TR has a cache miss. Thanks.
author: ivov

body:
I ran a few builds on this branch, and on master. To invalidate the build cache I also ran `tsc --build --clean` in every module.

For the `cli` package:

| Build | Time (seconds) |
| --- | --- |
| master | 20 |
| Incremental - no cache |  20 |
| Incremental - with cache | 5 |


![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/196144/187200890-09ce62a8-bac7-4dfe-80ac-eeca0670b867.png)

For the `nodes-base` package:

| Build | Time (seconds) |
| --- | --- |
| master | 60 |
| Incremental (no cache) |  62 |
| Incremental (with cache) | 9 |

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/196144/187203286-24a43fa9-f96e-42c2-ab5d-4c28fcb4b2f5.png)


For the full build (turbo repo cache disabled):

On master:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/196144/187207302-e34a6de6-4d64-4619-9063-877653b4f2aa.png)

On this branch, after the tsc cache is generated:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/196144/187207577-92f2fafb-0969-4d73-b05c-99a625784b22.png)

body:
Great, thanks a lot! Could you please also change that the node uses:
`this.helpers.requestWithAuthentication.call(this, credentialType, options)`
instead of
`await this.executeFunctions.helpers.request!(options)` [here](https://github.com/n8n-io/n8n/blob/master/packages/nodes-base/nodes/Rundeck/RundeckApi.ts#L36) and remove the headers. That makes sure that the node actually uses the authentication logic defined in the credentials instead of using its own logic. Else it could result in the test working fine but the node actually failing (as they do different things).

You can find an example [here](https://github.com/n8n-io/n8n/blob/master/packages/nodes-base/nodes/Ghost/GenericFunctions.ts#L46).

Thanks a lot!

body:
Hey @ruanjf,

I have just been having a play with this one and while the fix works what we can do instead is change the fallback value of the `getNodeParameter` call to use `null` instead of `0`. This feels a bit cleaner than using `undefined`, Do you have any objections to this approach instead?
author: Joffcom

body: @Joffcom I have no opinion. Previously, `null` was used but a TypeScript checked exception was thrown. Now I changed the code back to `null`. `Max Number of Requests` default value `0` also not work. body:
Hey @nikozila,

Thanks for the contribution, We recently released our Community Node Repository which is our preferred method for new node submissions.

It also allows users to gain access to your node and any updates without needing to update n8n itself.

You can find more information on building community nodes here: https://docs.n8n.io/integrations/community-nodes/build-community-nodes/

Let me know if you have any questions on this one.
author: Joffcom

I will take a look.

body:
> Builds fine, runs fine, only minor comments.
> 
>     1. Let's remove all tsx references? `"src/**/*.tsx"` `"tests/**/*.tsx",`, etc.

Done :heavy_check_mark: 

> 
>     2. `useUnknownInCatchVariables` Comment to enable in future?
enabled it globally, and disabled it on projects that needed it. So now the flag is under a `TODO` now.

>     3. Settings like `strictNullChecks`, `noImplicitAny`, etc. are part of `strict`, which is enabled everywhere except cli. Maybe a comment to remove these settings that are part of `strict` once we have `strict` all over?

I tried moving those to the `cli` tsconfig, but couldn't get it to work. I'll prioritize switching on `strict` for `cli`. we can remove these then. 

>     4. If we only need `emitDecoratorMetadata` and `experimentalDecorators` for TypeORM and for your router refactorings in cli, should we set them there only?


Done :heavy_check_mark: 

>     5. `"outDir": "dist"` is repeated in all packages. This is not inheritable?

[unfortunately not](https://github.com/microsoft/TypeScript/issues/29172). 
From what I understand, `outDir` is relative to the tsconfig defining it.
author: netroy

body:
> Rebuilt, retested, all good.
> 
>     6. Do we need to build the tests? If I remove them from tsconfig, the test command runs fine, maybe ts-jest transpiles them on the fly.

exactly, `ts-jest` transpiles the tests. we don't need to compile them.

>     7. When saving the tsconfigs locally, they are getting autoformatted with Prettier. If you can please format them and set up the extension `dangmai.workspace-default-settings` so your editor uses `.vscode/settings.default.json` to do this automatically in future.

I'll send another PR where I enable prettier & prettier-eslint on everything. Will reformat all JSON files in there as well.

> 
> Let's wait for Alex but otherwise LGTM. Thanks for cleaning this up.

author: netroy

body:
> 1. I would expect the npm package name to be `eslint-config-n8n` like others in npm, e.g. `eslint-config-airbnb`. Why the `@n8n-io/` namespace? Do we want to start publishing under this in future?

We already have the [`@n8n_io` org on npm](https://www.npmjs.com/org/n8n_io), and we have published there in the past. But this package is private, and only a dev-dependency. It'll not be published.


>     2. I would simplify the workspace/dir name to `eslint-config` because it is clear from context that it is for n8n and to remain consistent with other workspaces. For example, we use `nodes-base` for workspace name and `n8n-nodes-base` for npm package name.

The current setup with many of our package folder names not being the same as the package name, is an anti-pattern. I'd like us to become more consistent with how other monorepos do this, to reduce confusion for the contributors.
But, that's a bigger discussion, that I'll like to start a thread about elsewhere later.

>     3. Getting this on `npm run lint` at root:
> ```
> n8n-design-system:lint: Tried to lint /Users/ivov/Development/n8n/packages/design-system/src/locale/lang/en.js but found no valid, enabled rules for this file type and file path in the resolved configuration.
> ```

This is coming from tslint. Unrelated to the changes in this PR.


>     4. Getting also this on `npm run lint` at root:
> 
> 
> ```
> n8n-nodes-base:lint:
> n8n-nodes-base:lint: Oops! Something went wrong! :(
> n8n-nodes-base:lint:
> n8n-nodes-base:lint: ESLint: 8.23.0
> n8n-nodes-base:lint:
> n8n-nodes-base:lint: Error: Failed to load plugin 'eslint-plugin-n8n-nodes-base' declared in '.eslintrc.js#overrides[1]': Cannot find module '/Users/ivov/Development/n8n/packages/nodes-base/node_modules/eslint'
> n8n-nodes-base:lint: Require stack:
> n8n-nodes-base:lint: - /Users/ivov/Development/n8n/node_modules/eslint-docgen/src/rule-tester.js
> n8n-nodes-base:lint: - /Users/ivov/Development/n8n/node_modules/eslint-plugin-n8n-nodes-base/dist/lib/ast/utils/rule.js
> n8n-nodes-base:lint: - /Users/ivov/Development/n8n/node_modules/eslint-plugin-n8n-nodes-base/dist/lib/ast/utils/index.js
> n8n-nodes-base:lint: - /Users/ivov/Development/n8n/node_modules/eslint-plugin-n8n-nodes-base/dist/lib/rules/community-package-json-author-email-still-default.js
> n8n-nodes-base:lint: - /Users/ivov/Development/n8n/node_modules/eslint-plugin-n8n-nodes-base/dist/index.js
> n8n-nodes-base:lint: - /Users/ivov/Development/n8n/node_modules/@eslint/eslintrc/dist/eslintrc.cjs
> ```

Yeah. this is why the CI is failing. I'm trying to fix it. but in vain so far. It's caused by [this weird hack in eslint-docgen](https://github.com/wikimedia/eslint-docgen/blob/master/src/rule-tester.js#L5).

>     5. Getting this when opening TS files:
> 
> 
> ![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/44588767/188900170-65e69dc2-3724-41fe-85c8-c0e01bb89b2e.png)

This is fixed by https://github.com/n8n-io/n8n/pull/4049


>     6. Ticket number in PR description should be 4553.

:+1: 

>     7. Would it be reasonable to pin the ESLint version so we can very deliberate when upgrading it both here and in the linter? Changes in the ESLint AST have caused linter checks to fail in the past: https://linear.app/n8n/issue/N8N-3968

:+1: 

>     8. Do you know of a way to detect lint exceptions i.e. `eslint-disable-next-line` that no longer have effect?
not aware of a way. but I can investigate. My plan was to get rid of the most of the `eslint-disable-*` exceptions.
author: netroy

body:
> It's caused by [this weird hack in eslint-docgen](https://github.com/wikimedia/eslint-docgen/blob/master/src/rule-tester.js#L5).

Does it help if I remove `eslint-docgen` from the linter's published files?

The linter uses `eslint-docgen` to wrap ESLint's native `RuleTester` to generate lint rule docs, so `eslint-docgen` does not belong in its regular deps. But the linter's `import/no-extraneous-dependencies` flags this: `'eslint-docgen' should be listed in the project's dependencies, not devDependencies`

If removing it helps, I can do it now and later look into why this is being flagged, maybe related to the issue you linked to.
author: ivov

body:

But the linter's `import/no-extraneous-dependencies` flags this: `'eslint-docgen' should be listed in the project's dependencies, not devDependencies`
> 
> If removing it helps, I can do it now and later look into why this is being flagged, maybe related to the issue you linked to.

I think removing `eslint-docgen` will most likely help.

body:
> One lingering concern. On refreshing or returning to the canvas, `master` is perceptively faster than the Vite branch on 5678 and 8080, although metrics like `DOMContentLoaded` are much better.

@ivov Only the first few loads will be slower until Vite creates the dependencies graph and caches it. Subsequent starts are much faster than webpack. At least that's how it is for me.

author: OlegIvaniv

body: @ivov Such a helpful review and on-point comments, thank you! 🙏🏻 I now pushed an updated version where I did my best to take your advice into account: - renamed the rule into `no-uncaught-json-parse` (good name, thanks!) - now using your `eslint-plugin-n8n-local-rules`, and serving `local-rules.js` from within `packages/@n8n_io/eslint-config` - restructured the rule to start at a `CallExpression` and not at the deeper `MemberExpression`, to minimize parent lookups - added `Identifier` checks as suggested, what we would've had done if it was in Typescript (the rule is still in Javascript, see notes on TS below) - added a minimal (local) test suite `local-rules.test.js` that can be run with `npm test` - enabled the rule at the "warn" level in `eslint-config/base.js` Following todays linting configuration updates, this will only now get triggered for diffs, so to test it one will need to create a new `JSON.parse(foo)` call somewhere and save the file once. What I would do separately, out of the scope of this PR: - introduce a setup for easily including TS-built custom rules and their tests within the monorepo, including converting this one into Typescript (N8N-4718) - create a tech-debt issue for fixing the offending JSON.parse() calls in the current codebase

body: We used a regex in the past to solve other security issues. It caused a lot of problems (mainly broke workflows because of false positives) and we then had to roll it back. Is there no other way of fixing this?
author: janober

body:
@janober I thought about changing the prototype but then it would also leak to the main code.

This is only preventing `.` followed by `constructor` with or without spaces. So if you have a sentece such as `This is a good example. Constuctor is a cool thing.` will not be denied because of the uppercase `C` in `Constructor` (and `Math.floor.Constructor(...)` is not an issue).

body:
> 1. URL is case-insensitive so the link will work, but should we be allowing for this casing divergence? `n8n-nodes-base.activecampaigntrigger` vs. `n8n-nodes-base.activeCampaignTrigger` The canonical name is camelCased after the prefix. No immediate risk that I can think of, but I would avoid this divergence if we can help it.

Our hosting automatically lowercases URLs (this is the background to making the links lowercase)

body:
> @agobrech will this replace the Cron node?

Hi @luizeof, the scheduler is a combination of the Cron node and the Interval Node with some added features. Workflows that still have the Cron node or the Interval node will still work. You won't be able to find the old Cron node and the old Interval node in the node menu.

can we perhaps we merge the two implementations to avoid inconsistent behaviors?

author: CLAassistant

body:
Hey @ggagrinas,

Thanks for the contribution, We have now created a Community Node Repository which is our preferred method for new node submissions.

Once a node has been published to the Community Node Repository users will be able to install the node from their n8n interface and will be able to use your node quicker, You will also be able to update the node as needed without needing to wait for the review process.

In the future we may reach out to you about including your node in our main release, But for now I am going to close this PR.

You can find more information in the links below, If you have any questions about this or need any help let us know.

Building Community Nodes: https://docs.n8n.io/integrations/community-nodes/build-community-nodes/

Submitting Community Nodes: https://docs.n8n.io/integrations/creating-nodes/deploy/submit-community-nodes/

Published Nodes: https://www.npmjs.com/search?q=keywords:n8n-community-node-package
author: Joffcom

body:
@Joffcom Thank you for the fast response. Is there an estimated time for reviewing our node?
Community node is already implemented but we want our clients to have the easiest way of using our node.
author: ggagrinas

body:
Hey @ggagrinas,

Nodes are not really reviewed as such, There are some internal metrics we use for the community nodes to work out which nodes would be suited towards becoming a core node. At that stage we will reach out to have a chat with you about making it an official node.

body:
Great PR! Please pay attention to the following items before merging:

__Files matching `packages/**`:__
- [ ] If fixing bug, added test to cover scenario.
- [ ] If addressing forum or Github issue, added link to description.

__Files matching `packages/**/*.ts`:__
- [ ] Added unit tests to cover new or updated functionality.

__Files matching `**/*.vue`:__
- [ ] Used composition API for all new components.
- [ ] Added component or unit tests to cover functionality.

__Files matching `packages/editor-ui/**/*.vue`:__
- [ ] Added E2E if adding new features.
- [ ] Used design system tokens (colors, spacings...) where possible.

__Files matching `packages/editor-ui/src/views/NodeView.vue`:__
- [ ] Avoided adding code here. Only refactored to make it smaller.

__Files matching `packages/nodes-base/nodes/**`:__
- [ ] Added workflow tests for nodes if possible.

__Files matching `packages/nodes-base/package.json`:__
- [ ] Avoided adding dependencies for nodes if not absolutely necessary.


Make sure to check off this list before asking for review.
author: github-actions

body:
Things that we need to address before this can go out of beta
1. running pyodide in an isolated context, like we do with JS code using `vm2`
2. once in isolated context, try to re-use the context, to avoid the slowdown due to pyodide init being slow.
3. setup a new branch called `python-code-beta` where we set the default code node version to `2`, and release a custom docker image for beta-testers.

body:
Hey @bdelcamp,

Thanks for the PR, This is something that I have felt we have been missing for a while. I guess we will need to wait for the other PR to be merged first.

There are a couple of tweaks for this one as well, There is the toggle for SSL/TLS but it is just set to true for the credential test instead of using the credential value. It would also be worth adding a toggle to ignore invalid / self signed certificates as well and setting secureOptions to include rejectUnauthorized.

I do very much like what you have started to do here though, Nice work 🙌🏻 

... quick edit: I wonder if it would be worth changing the toggle to a dropdown so we can have Plain, Implicit and Explicit as options.
author: Joffcom
