Hi guys, 
Thanks @bdelcamp @Joffcom for your great work.
IT could be great to have SSL/TLS to FTP node soon...
author: j2bd

body: It doesn't seem like promise-ftp will be updated, not sure what other alternative there would be other than to fork it and include it directly...
author: bdelcamp

body: @bdelcamp please let me know if you plan to create a fork. We also have an interest in upgrading the dependencies to the latest version to remove some deprecation warnings and possible vulnerabilities.
author: flosky
IceBreaker8

body:
> @bdelcamp I have clone the fix you made, npm install, npm run build and npm run start, but the problem isn't fixed for me. We are trying to establish an FTP connection to an outside server via n8n. When we do so, we get an SSL/TLS connection error.

@IceBreaker8  Did you see that it also requires a patched version of [realtymaps/promise-ftp](https://github.com/realtymaps/promise-ftp/pull/47) ?
author: bdelcamp

body:
> @bdelcamp please let me know if you plan to create a fork. We also have an interest in upgrading the dependencies to the latest version to remove some deprecation warnings and possible vulnerabilities.

@flosky I don't know if I can devote the time to maintaining a fork of that library, I would propose that the n8n project create the fork, that way anyone can directly contribute to it, and it can be directly included in the n8n builds.
author: bdelcamp

body: @bdelcamp I did upgrade that dependency manually... Do you have a working github project repo that works that I can clone?
author: IceBreaker8

body:
@bdelcamp my worry there is I don't know if we would have the time to maintain a fork of the library either.

I have some other thoughts though which I will look into early next year.
author: Joffcom

body: @Joffcom To be fair, it's really not maintained at present either, but if the project forks it, then any contributor can create pull requests against it to keep it relevant to the project itself.
author: bdelcamp
morphar

body:
I don't know exactly what to expect with regards to errors, but I have set up a dev env and tried adding the above `overrides` to [package.json in nodes-base](https://github.com/n8n-io/n8n/blob/master/packages/nodes-base/package.json) and the changes made in this pull request.
So far I've succeeded in listing files and downloading a file from an FTPS server.

Just to be on the safe side: I am aware of the difference between SFTP and FTPS ;-)
And I am 100% sure that I am now talking to an FTPS server that I could not connect with, before these updates.
author: morphar

body: @morphar Good idea, that didn't even occur to me... This seems like the most maintainable solution at the moment until the upstream maintainer wants to update their dependencies.

author: dncpax

body:
Hey @dncpax,

This is on my radar but it is something that I think needs to be done as part of an overhaul so we can make sure we support both Implicit and Explicit FTPS and some of the other options. The best way I can think of to do this would be to change to a different package which won't be a quick job.
author: Joffcom

body:
Would also be interested in this.
I'm self-hosting this in docker, would the fixes from @bdelcamp and @morphar be something that could be changed on a running container as a temporary fix or does it require a full rebuild from source?
author: Nionor

body:
Hey @bdelcamp,

It looks like the PR has still not been merged on the other end, What I have done internally is created a dev ticket to move to a different package so that we can implement this properly in the future.

Because of this planned change it does sadly mean that I will need to close this PR as it will no longer be relevant as such, Thanks for sticking with us on this one.

I don't have a timeline yet but I want to make sure we get this done this year.

body:
Hey @jrCleber,

Thanks for the contribution, We have now created a Community Node Repository which is our preferred method for new node submissions, It looks like you have already published this node to npm so it can be used already which is great.

Once a node has been published to the Community Node Repository users will be able to install the node from their n8n interface and will be able to use your node quicker, You will also be able to update the node as needed without needing to wait for the review process.

In the future we may reach out to you about including your node in our main release, But for now I am going to close this PR.

You can find more information in the links below, If you have any questions about this or need any help let us know.

Building Community Nodes: https://docs.n8n.io/integrations/community-nodes/build-community-nodes/
Submitting Community Nodes: https://docs.n8n.io/integrations/creating-nodes/deploy/submit-community-nodes/
Published Nodes: https://www.npmjs.com/search?q=keywords:n8n-community-node-package

body:
`pnpm` author is agains [Open Intenet principals ](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Net_neutrality#Open_Internet) and blocks access to developers from multiple countries:
https://github.com/pnpm/pnpm/issues/4800
https://github.com/pnpm/pnpm/issues/5626
https://github.com/pnpm/pnpm/issues/4498

I've spent an hour to find out a real reason why `n8n` can not be installed on my local machine. And cause of problem was in *pnpm* package manager. At the end I enabled VPN to overcome the problem. 

IMHO - the Internet should be Opened for all individuals whenever from his residence, region and personaliy. So I recommend to not use **pnpm** package manager in **n8n** and switch to other well working alternatives like yarn or bit.
author: borisbsu

body:
@borisbsu You don't need pnpm to install n8n. pnpm is only needed for our development setup. We tried yarn first, as many of us have prior experience with yarn, but yarn doesn't have an equivalent of `--omit-dev` since version 2.x, which leads to us having to ship dev dependencies in our docker builds. 

However we do agree with you that the Internet should be open to app, and that's why the we provide [multiple ways to install n8n](https://docs.n8n.io/choose-n8n/), none of which require the end-user to even know what pnpm is.

__Files matching `packages/**`:__

__Files matching `packages/**/*.ts`:__

body:
> @netroy I added `console.warn` only with path to prop. Would it be fine? looks like this (taken from unit test) 

This should be fine for now. I'll replace it with a warning that gets sents to sentry after https://github.com/n8n-io/n8n/pull/4394 is merged.
author: netroy

body:
After further testing and also trying out [structuredClone](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/structuredClone) native function (@RicardoE105 recommended it to my attention but available only from node >=17.0.0 ) turned out using `WeakMap` is the most efficient way of cloning objects which have circular references (slightly faster than using `WeakSet`)

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5410822/198739782-eecbda4d-0b61-4cb1-9280-f0b4d5a57393.png)

Without circular refs `deepCopy` wins of course but with using `WeakMap` still comes to the 2nd place

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5410822/198741171-b7aa022a-6101-414e-be84-4349230de33b.png)
author: cstuncsik

author: netroy

body:
> Any reason not to keep `n8n-custom` image at 16 like the others?
> 
> Otherwise LGTM.

Using the `n8n-custom` image as a test for node 18. That way we can have the nightly image use node 18 for a while before we switch everything over to 18.
author: netroy

body:
- [DB tests pass](https://github.com/n8n-io/n8n/actions/runs/3397211474/jobs/5649132810).
- [Docker image works](https://hub.docker.com/layers/n8nio/n8n/N8N-5129-N8N-5130-N8N-4442/images/sha256-48a1a92422ee57af60ef16a587e5f27a22097384ecb86bfe696cd94f375c98d7?context=explore)
- [Hooks updated](https://gitlab.com/n8n1/backend/-/merge_requests/128)

>     1. From a clean install, I get this issue on `build`
>     2. If I ts-ignore the above issue, `build` finishes and I get this issue on `start`
>     3. Failing tests in `cli` on sqlite

Can you please try these again?



>     4. We should aim for consistency in wildcard imports vs named imports, e.g. `import * as ActiveExecutions from '~/ActiveExecutions'` vs `import { ActiveExecutions } from '~/ActiveExecutions'`. I'd personally prefer named imports.
I personally prefer named imports as well, but perhaps that can be another PR?



>     5. We should aim for consistency in index imports. `import { X } from '~/index'` vs `import { X } from '..'`. I'd personally prefer the more explicit variant.
I'd prefer that we never use `~/index`. it's the largest culprit behind cyclic dependencies.
I deleted that file, and make the `cli` compile, test, and run without it. I've added the file back for backward compatibility. But, it's not used anymore.



>     6. We should aim for consistency in relative paths. `import config from './config'` vs `import config from '~/config'`. I'd personally prefer package-relative paths.
Agreed, and done. I also changed `~/` to `@/`, to be consistent with what the frontend code is using.



>     7. Should we lint to enforce that all relative paths are package-relative paths instead of regular relative paths? Can come later.
Might be worth trying, but only if we see people not using package-relative imports.



>     8. Some more path aliases might be useful, e.g. `~/databases/entities` → `@models`. Can come later.
Added `@db/` for `src/database/` as a first example. We could add more like `@um/` for `src//UserManagement/` in another PR.
author: netroy

body:
> Seeing multiple type issues from cross-package imports. From a clean install and after restarting TS language server.

looks like an issue with eslint https://github.com/typescript-eslint/typescript-eslint/issues/2094 . Let me see if I can find a workaround

> > I also changed `~/` to `@/`, to be consistent with what the frontend code is using.
> 
> Matter of preference but I find it better to keep `@` for path shortcuts and `~` for package root, because `~` is clear in meaning and because using `@` for both package root and for shortcuts can lead to confusion: `@/someDir` vs `@someDir`. We can also change FE after discussion.

I'd prefer to make that another PR where we change the FE and BE code together.
author: netroy

body: Looks like the eslint errors aren't due to project references, but because of the package-relative imports (or at-least how they are setup).

>     2. New command `typecheck` fails.

Yeah, I'm aware. I added the command to iterate quicker, and to also type-check the tests without having to run them. Unfortunately we have typescript errors in frontend code that'd I'd like fix in a separate PR.

>     3. Should we remove the Netlify cred or add it to the node? Why is it dangling?

Do you mean `NetlifyOAuth2Api.credentials.ts`? that file seems like it was [added a year ago](https://github.com/n8n-io/n8n/pull/2177/files#diff-40ed1b25aee9f2180ad40d217267e66953a1a034bbd809f693074a45b36c5533), and has been commented out since the start. Since it isn't used, I deleted it.
author: netroy

body:
> 1. This breaks the dev flow for node builders in the team and in the community, because `watch` transpiles changes to dist but does not reflect them in the root dir cache. We have to keep the cache fresh or bypass it for `watch`.

Done. for now, we'll be using `tsc-watch` to regenerate the types after `tsc` finishes successfully. This however does not update the already-loaded nodes in a running server, and nor does it restart the server. But, that's the same behavior on `master` as well.
I'd like to make the type-generation script incremental, and would also like to automatically unload nodes/credential classes from a running server, so we don't actually need to restart the server. But, that's another big change, and I'd rather not do that in this PR.
We don't need to worry about refreshing the `known` files for now either as we still need to add files names explicitly in the `n8n` block in the package.json. I plan to tackle that as well soon.

> * In `/dist/known/credentials.json` the mapping from credential type name to `className` and `sourcePath` looks inferrable. Do we need these two details to load a credential type? In `/dist/known/nodes.json`, the mapping from node type name to `className` looks inferrable but `sourcePath` becomes unpredictable because of grouping dirs like `Aws`, which btw I am not sure we benefit much from.

In `master` we are inferring `className` from the `fileName`. I changed this to avoid additional string manipulation and garbage generation at startup. But, I'm not sure if the impact of that is significant. If you prefer, I can remove `className` from the generated files, and infer it before setting it in the objects stores in `this.known` in `/home/aditya/Work/n8n/n8n/packages/cli/src/LoadNodesAndCredentials.ts`. WDYT?

body:
Hey @Daemonxiao,

Thanks for the contribution, We have now created a Community Node Repository which is our preferred method for new node submissions.

Once a node has been published to the Community Node Repository users will be able to install the node from their n8n interface and will be able to use your node quicker, You will also be able to update the node as needed without needing to wait for the review process.

In the future we may reach out to you about including your node in our main release, But for now I am going to close this PR.

You can find more information in the links below, If you have any questions about this or need any help let us know.

Building Community Nodes: https://docs.n8n.io/integrations/community-nodes/build-community-nodes/

Submitting Community Nodes: https://docs.n8n.io/integrations/creating-nodes/deploy/submit-community-nodes/

Published Nodes: https://www.npmjs.com/search?q=keywords:n8n-community-node-package

N8N-5409 Implement date parser

In json dates are strings or numbers (timestamps). There is a convention about using [ISO 8601](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601) format but there is no guarantee. The parser would need to recognize at least some of the most common formats by testing the value with regex

author: linear

body:
N8N-5343 Dynamically delay evaluation resolution

Each time the value of a parameter changes (char added, char removed, text cut or pasted), delay for 150ms before calculating new result. If new result introduces a **new** error (i.e. don’t count existing ones in the expression), delay for another 150ms before calculating new result. This should reduce # of times user sees an error when crafting an expr manually. Make sure these timeouts are easy to change, we’ll likely have to hone them in during review. Since user might be typing quickly, it’s still good to give them feedback. After 5x subsequent delays in a row, force render the result without a delay.

author: CLAassistant

body:
@ivov I would say this is all expected. Here's the reasoning:

**1., 2. and 4.** These popups were discussed when implementing this feature and we decided to keep all messages from the old execution preview. I just now talked with Max and the agreement is to remove the _Failed execution_ messages that appear and pile up when opening failed executions. Also we agreed to be conservative with removing popups so we don't remove anything useful. Deleting mentioned popup will already clear a lot of clutter so I'll do this in next commit.

**3.** I don't have much context on this, but retrying stopped executions hasn't been possible in previous versions also (just tested `0.198.2`). Will ask around to see why is this but for now would leave it out of scope for this particular PR.

**5.** Executions tab always remembers the last selected execution, so  that one will be automatically opened when switching to it (or the first one if it's opened for the first time or remembered execution is not longer available).

Let me know if this all seems reasonable.

Great PR! Please pay attention to the following items before merging:

__Files matching `packages/**`:__
- [ ] If fixing bug, added test to cover scenario.
- [ ] If addressing forum or Github issue, added link to description.

__Files matching `packages/**/*.ts`:__
- [ ] Added unit tests to cover new or updated functionality.

__Files matching `**/*.vue`:__
- [ ] Used composition API for all new components.
- [ ] Added component or unit tests to cover functionality.

__Files matching `packages/editor-ui/**/*.vue`:__
- [ ] Added E2E if adding new features.
- [ ] Used design system tokens (colors, spacings...) where possible.


Make sure to check off this list before asking for review.

body:
A bit of context that may be helpful:
We are still using `NodeView` to display executions preview, it's just that it's used inside an `iframe` on _Executions_ tab. So [`openExecution`](https://github.com/n8n-io/n8n/blob/c2da21621fb08c676bd3dc5e2a9de613a4f135f3/packages/editor-ui/src/views/NodeView.vue#L682) function is still needed, but it's only used [here](https://github.com/n8n-io/n8n/blob/c2da21621fb08c676bd3dc5e2a9de613a4f135f3/packages/editor-ui/src/views/NodeView.vue#L3163), so all of [this logic](https://github.com/n8n-io/n8n/blob/c2da21621fb08c676bd3dc5e2a9de613a4f135f3/packages/editor-ui/src/views/NodeView.vue#L2182) in `initView` can go away (which was already done in this PR 🙌). Another thing is the `isExecutionPreview` data member which is used to distinguish `NodeView` used to display workflows from the ones used to preview executions, for now we use it only to set canvas background but it's important none the less.

For reference, this is how canvas should look like:

![SCR-20221119-fmm](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/2598782/202846366-54a7b99e-cb35-49fa-b9fd-c4bfc664a23e.png)

And this is what I see on this branch

![SCR-20221119-fnv](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/2598782/202846380-30a9d5e5-fc7a-4d7e-903b-97cc810be882.png)


And this is what I see on this branch:

body: instead of making an exception for `E-goi`, should we change the rule to enfore the name that you get after removing certain characters like `-`?
author: netroy

body: [`titleCase`](https://github.com/blakeembrey/change-case/blob/master/packages/title-case/src/index.ts) in the plugin capitalizes all words except articles and prepositions and conjunctions, whether hyphenated or not. This matches [traditional rules](https://www.proofreadnow.com/blog/bid/94159/Hyphenation-in-Titles) for casing of hyphenated nouns and adjectives in titles, which makes me think hyphenated brand names in nodes-base (rare as they are, no other example) are likely to follow the pattern, making `E-goi` an exception, but hard for me to say. Maybe @StarfallProjects knows better.
author: ivov

body:
I think Ivan's approach will be right in most cases. The site he links describes a common style convention for title case headings (I think it may be drawn from the Chicago Manual of Style? Don't quote me on that though). 

Conventions vary depending on what style guide you follow. Are we basing our UI copy styles on any particular style guide? Worth noting that a lot of popular tech style guides (i.e. guides focused on digital rather than print, including Google's and Microsoft's) avoid title case altogether, but that's a separate issue: main thing is we're consistent, and matching brand names exactly.
author: StarfallProjects

body:
> Are we basing our UI copy styles on any particular style guide?

Mostly David's indications on top of Jan's preexisting choices. David is not a fan of title case either but we kept it because it was so prevalent.
author: ivov

body:
Great PR! Please pay attention to the following items before merging:

__Files matching `packages/**`:__
- [ ] If fixing bug, added test to cover scenario.
- [ ] If addressing forum or Github issue, added link to description.

__Files matching `packages/**/*.ts`:__
- [ ] Added unit tests to cover new or updated functionality.

__Files matching `packages/cli/src/databases/migrations/**`:__
- [ ] Requested review from at least two engineers on migration.
- [ ] Avoided irreversible data migrations.
- [ ] Avoided deleting or updating data keys.
- [ ] Wrote 'down' migration if possible.

__Files matching `cypress/e2e/**`:__
- [ ] Avoided chaining commands more than two or three times (to avoid flakiness because only last one will be retried).
- [ ] Spoofed endpoints that are not critical for the test (to avoid flakiness).
- [ ] Picked most efficient path to start the test (for example skipped account setup and starting at /workflow/new for a canvas test).
- [ ] Avoided adding waits on time (use request intercepts instead).
- [ ] Ensured each spec does not depend on any another spec to pass.


Make sure to check off this list before asking for review.

Hey @tinti,

This PR looks interesting and I know we have had some issues reported about duplicated trigger events from the MQTT node so it sounds like this may fix those.

Did you find a way to reproduce duplicated trigger events to test if it works?
author: Joffcom

body:
> Hey @tinti,

Hi @Joffcom 

> This PR looks interesting and I know we have had some issues reported about duplicated trigger events from the MQTT node so it sounds like this may fix those.
> 
> Did you find a way to reproduce duplicated trigger events to test if it works?

I can guarantee that it has solved the issue with my use case.

Reproducing at my environment is simple. By starting https://hub.docker.com/_/eclipse-mosquitto and adding 3 clients sending events every 30 seconds each is enough to trigger duplicated events.

Without the patch multiple events are created. With it, there is no duplicated events.

@Joffcom
Is there a way, to split the Descriptions based on a node-parameter, like here called "apiVersion"?

If not, I would like to split the current node into 2 nodes. (Invoice Ninja V4 & Invoice Ninja V5), because the differ to much from one another and are hardly to manage both at the same time.
I've tried to split them based on the Description parameters, but it seems not to integrate well into the n8n system. (like attached)
- operations get displayed multiple times
- in eighter way, all oprations are always visible, no matter, which apiVersion is selected  

Please present me the optimal next steps, to make this separation happen. Does the exsisting node have to be deprecated and 2 separate new Nodes to be created? Or do you prefer anything else?
[InvoiceNinja.zip](https://github.com/n8n-io/n8n/files/10499594/InvoiceNinja.zip)
author: paulwer

body:
Hey @paulwer,

There shouldn't be any need to make 2 nodes as we support v4 and v5 in the node through the node versioning, If you have a setting that only exists for v5 you can set the display the display to only show for that version and the same for any data structures that are different you can use the `apiVersion` to work out what endpoint to use.

If you look at the current node you can see where we are already doing this.

author: Joffcom

body:
Alright I have taken a quick look, It looks like the Trigger node and the app node are no longer being treated as the same node in the UI so it appears twice, The trigger node was also versioned although I suspect that isn't actually needed.

Looking at the node we currently have we toggle between v4 and v5 under the settings which can also impact UI fields but now I can see the node itself I can see the extra fields and I get that it was just easier to create a new version but it is not needed and the v2 of the node also contains the same changes so it seems a bit over the top.

The `Additional JSON Body` that appears on all of the operations can probably be hidden behind an additional fields option (which is normally the last option on a node).

The notice is nice but maybe not needed and the V4 / V5 next to the resource can also be removed, Looking at the changes that need to be made to it this it will probably take a day or 2.

The actual implementation of the API options and fields looks to be mostly ok it is just the way the versioning has been handled that needs to be sorted so that fits our current patterns. The downside here though is this node currently sits outside of our top 100 so getting any prioritisation on making these changes will be tricky and possibly unlikely so I may have to work on this between other issues to bring it back inline.
author: Joffcom

:)
author: paulwer

body:
We don't have anything written down as such for versioning as we never really expected the community to do full versioning it is more something we would play with.

I think the main thing to do would be to revert the full versioning changes then work on adding the changes to the current node.
author: Joffcom

body:
I guess I can take my hands on this. I first want to describe my changes and the ideas behind it and second want to nail the next steps, which are required from me.

**Intentions**
While development I had 2 goals to fullfill with my "new" structure. 
1. **Decoupleing IN-API-Versions**: The old codebase has programmcode combined for v4 and v5 of IN. Functions only determine at runntime which version and what to send to the api while having the same input-fields. I want to decouple this for 2 reasons:
- fields may differ between the two IN-versions
- better overview as a devoloper and therefore likly less errors and better testing
2. **Multiple N8N-Node Versions**: As a result of the decoupling described above, i wanted to provide backward-compatibility, to ensure nothing existing breaks, after my changes. This was needed because in the v5 version multiple parameters had changed and are not called like in the n8n-node version before. f.ex. positions within an invoice are called completly different and it was 1 field more. (if I remember it correctly)

**Please describe next steps...**
Do you require to just remove the old n8n-versions or do i have to merge v4 and v5 back into each other, to use the same parameter Names, like they were called before?
author: paulwer

body:
Hey @paulwer,

I currently do not have a timeline for this PR, I have now set up 2 slots each week for reviewing PRs but the order depends on when the PR came in and the complexity required to review.

As this PR requires a lot more than a quick review it will take longer, the problem is trying to fit something of this size into a normal review slot when we typically reserve this kind of thing for a node overhaul.

My plan for the node is to take the current node from the master branch then add in the changes to the light version with out all the extra complexity.

Hey @paulwer,

I have some of the changes in place but due to the scale it is still tricky to fit this in, Sadly this PR got way out of hand and didn't follow earlier recommendations which is part of why it is taking so long to complete.

author: paulwer

body:
Hey @paulwer,

You are not wrong there this has been opened for a lot longer than I expected from the initial version all those months ago.

Sadly though the scope of the changes made and the direction taken just hasn't made this easy or quick to fit in around everything else.

Normally something of this size would be closed with a note to break it up into smaller chunks and to follow the pattern of the current node.

We might have been ok with the first few commits before it went off the rails then we could have had a chat about to overhaul the node properly.

The issue right now is I just lack the time to fix and adjust everything so I am having to fit it in between other tasks.

If you wanted to take a bash at it we need to revert the entire second version you created and fit it into the current light version then we can think about if it is worth doing a full version using newer ui components.

There are multiple unexpected errors being logged in tests because of the `.catch(error => console.log(error))` lines.
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/196144/210271964-e1925c91-60ad-463f-9edb-1133711fb24a.png)

We should 
1. disable event logs in tests, except for where is makes sense
2. remove the `.catch` lines, as we can already see that they are not only catching actual promise errors but also errors from bad mocks, etc.

This is the bets I could manage.
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/196144/208109936-900315b2-7bfd-46f3-ac62-60ccc3683fed.png)

Perhaps we should look into modifying tmpl to allow adding metadata to the evaluated string, so that we can then use that show a warning labels above an evaluated expression.
author: netroy

body:
1. Should probably also render `[no preview available for $env expression]`, no? Is that an easy fix?
2. Agree - if I remember correctly, we don't have blue currently in our design system for info. Max used yellow for that in one of his design protos. Is changing the color here easy? Otherwise, I think we can live with green.
3. I think `[no preview available for $env expression]` is the best output we can have here because it doesn't matter what comes after the $env if we can't preview that
4. Agree
5. Should also result in `[no preview available for $env expression]` in my opinion
6. Same as (5)

In my opinion we should always show  `[no preview available for $env expression]` as soon as we have the $env.

author: github-actions

body:
Hi @Joffcom 
sry, for my late reply. I would suggest the following:

![Screenshot n8n file trigger](https://github.com/n8n-io/n8n/assets/13757952/f86c3495-79de-492d-9921-8ae9de9b16e0)

Remove files / folders option as it is useless, see my comment above: https://github.com/n8n-io/n8n/pull/4914#discussion_r1188741669

Add multiple paths, as we often need this and it follows a bit more the DRY principle.

Add options:
- Use Polling: As this is required if you want to work on a network storage like a samba share
- Await write finish (and stability threshold - only shown if awf == true): If the write process is a bit slow or pauses too long, for example scanning documents with a network scanner
- Skip empty files: Often empty files can be skipped

I would change the output to:
- name: name of the changed file or folder
- path: absolute path
- folders: array of the folders, as we often use this to process files depending on the subfolders
- extension
- nameWithoutExtension
- size
- modifiedTimestamp
- event: The filesystem event which triggered this output

What do you think about these modifications?
author: marzn

body:
Hey @marzn,

I am not against any of those changes although I would make sure any output changes doesn't impact what could be currently used. The tricky bit will be implementing the changes without introducing any breaking changes, I am not sure on the multiple paths as I have seen many issues over the years when using mutliple paths for folder monitors has gone horribly wrong so it would be best to keep that to just one for now.

Having said that though I know that we are looking an overhaul for a lot of the file nodes so maybe we could use this as a base for a new version of the trigger. I will drop this PR in front of the designer working on the new node and we can maybe get everything following the same pattern and released at the same time.

Hi @Joffcom ,

if I understand it correctly, it does not make sense to discuss higher level node design here, as we can not make a decision anyway?

That is totally fine for me, but in this case, I wont invest more time in this PR.

Feel free to merge this PR, as it is better than the current implementation, even it is not perfect which is stated in the discussion above. Otherwise please just close the PR and reopen the issue to track the problem, as in my opinion a PR indicates WIP, which is not the case here.

Thank you :)

body:
General questions to this approach:
- what if you cant determine the type of the request body based on the Content-Type header (for example, incorrect header? or if the type is generic)
author: whoiskatrin

body:
> General questions to this approach:
> 
> * what if you cant determine the type of the request body based on the Content-Type header (for example, incorrect header? or if the type is generic)

If we can't determine the type of the request body based on the Content-Type header, then we continue as usual, no change there.
I believe the default body type is plain text.
If that causes an error - very well. An incorrect Content-Type is a serious error by the client.

body: @Joffcom It's fully backwards compatible, it does not change anything about the api url or params construction, only how page param values are calculated through iterations.
author: adrian-martinez-vdshop

body: Also, as it is contained within strapiApiRequestAllItems function, changes only applies to Return All functionality, which is broken at the moment (unless you have 20 entries or less in Strapi for the requested api).

Hey @adrian-martinez-vdshop,

Perfect, I shall give it a test soon on earlier versions of 4 and 3 just to be safe but it looks good so far.
author: Joffcom

body:
I forgot to mention that functionality is broken only for api v4. Changes are enclosed within a `if (apiVersion === 'v4') {` statement, so they should not affect api v3, which uses [pagination by offset](https://docs.strapi.io/developer-docs/latest/developer-resources/database-apis-reference/rest/sort-pagination.html#pagination-by-offset) instead.

It looks like when api v4 support was added, api v3 code was taken as the base, pagination was switched from offset to page, but numeric parameters were forgotten to be adapted properly.

author: netroy

body:
Hi @netroy, thanks for your feedback. 

> Have you tested this in the queue mode?
 I assume that we need to update ioredis to 4.x or 5.x to be able to use any new redis features.

After verification, no need to update any package. The current versions are fully compatible without adjustment.

Current packages : 
  - packages/cli : 
    - ioredis@4.28.5
    - bull@3.19.0
      - including ioredis@4.27.0

The authentication feature has been include to ioredis@4.17.0 : https://github.com/luin/ioredis/pull/1130

----------------------------------------------------
# Some tests to ensure compatibility

## Config

My redis users for tests :
```sh
$ redis-cli
127.0.0.1:6379> acl list
1) "user default on nopass ~* &* +@all"
2) "user user-with-password on #a1159e9df3670d549d04524532629f5477ceb7deec9b45e47e8c009506ecb2c8 ~* &* +@all"
3) "user user-without-password on nopass ~* &* +@all"
```

## Tests

I added a log of redisConfig in Queue.js to show the config used in queue mode.

### Default mode  

```sh
$ pnpm worker

> n8n@0.209.3 worker /var/www/n8n
> ./packages/cli/bin/n8n worker

Starting n8n worker...
{ host: 'localhost', port: 6379, db: 80 }

n8n worker is now ready
 * Version: 0.209.3
 * Concurrency: 10
```

### Specific user without password

```sh
$ export QUEUE_BULL_REDIS_USERNAME=user-without-password 
$ pnpm worker

> n8n@0.209.3 worker /var/www/n8n
> ./packages/cli/bin/n8n worker

Starting n8n worker...
{
  host: 'localhost',
  username: 'user-without-password',
  port: 6379,
  db: 80
}

n8n worker is now ready
 * Version: 0.209.3
 * Concurrency: 10
```

### Specific user with password

```sh
$ export QUEUE_BULL_REDIS_USERNAME=user-with-password 
$ export QUEUE_BULL_REDIS_PASSWORD=pwd
$ pnpm worker

> n8n@0.209.3 worker /var/www/n8n
> ./packages/cli/bin/n8n worker

Starting n8n worker...
{
  host: 'localhost',
  username: 'user-with-password',
  password: 'pwd',
  port: 6379,
  db: 80
}

n8n worker is now ready
 * Version: 0.209.3
 * Concurrency: 10
```

### Rotten case : Specific user with bad password

```sh
$ export QUEUE_BULL_REDIS_PASSWORD=pwdbad
$ pnpm worker

> n8n@0.209.3 worker /var/www/n8n
> ./packages/cli/bin/n8n worker

Starting n8n worker...
{
  host: 'localhost',
  username: 'user-with-password',
  password: 'pwdbad',
  port: 6379,
  db: 80
}

n8n worker is now ready
 * Version: 0.209.3
 * Concurrency: 10

Error from queue: 

/var/www/n8n/node_modules/.pnpm/redis-parser@3.0.0/node_modules/redis-parser/lib/parser.js:179
    return new ReplyError(string)
           ^
ReplyError: WRONGPASS invalid username-password pair or user is disabled.
    at parseError (/var/www/n8n/node_modules/.pnpm/redis-parser@3.0.0/node_modules/redis-parser/lib/parser.js:179:12)
    at parseType (/var/www/n8n/node_modules/.pnpm/redis-parser@3.0.0/node_modules/redis-parser/lib/parser.js:302:14)
 ELIFECYCLE  Command failed with exit code 1.
```
### Rotten case : Unknow user

```sh
$ export QUEUE_BULL_REDIS_USERNAME=user-unknow          
$ pnpm worker

> n8n@0.209.3 worker /var/www/n8n
> ./packages/cli/bin/n8n worker

Starting n8n worker...
{ host: 'localhost', username: 'user-unknow', port: 6379, db: 80 }

n8n worker is now ready
 * Version: 0.209.3
 * Concurrency: 10

Error from queue: 

/var/www/n8n/node_modules/.pnpm/redis-parser@3.0.0/node_modules/redis-parser/lib/parser.js:179
    return new ReplyError(string)
           ^
ReplyError: WRONGPASS invalid username-password pair or user is disabled.
    at parseError (/var/www/n8n/node_modules/.pnpm/redis-parser@3.0.0/node_modules/redis-parser/lib/parser.js:179:12)
    at parseType (/var/www/n8n/node_modules/.pnpm/redis-parser@3.0.0/node_modules/redis-parser/lib/parser.js:302:14)
 ELIFECYCLE  Command failed with exit code 1.
```

body: Is currently sadly blocked as it is a breaking change. So we will probably release it with n8n version 1.0 .

author: Stockotaco

body:
> Is currently sadly blocked as it is a breaking change. So we will probably release it with n8n version 1.0

@janober to bad :X what is braking with this change?

Maybe we can look into implement both WebSockets and Server Side events and that it can be enabled via the .env.
The implementation looks a lot alike, and this way we keep it backwards compatible.
author: matthijs166

body:
What it would be breaking is all n8n instances that are currently using a reverse proxy in some form that is not configured to be able to handle WebSockets (which are most).

Exactly, what you proposed is also what we have talked about yesterday. Making it configurable (so supporting both),  having the default set to be SSE and to change that default with v1 to WebSocket.
author: janober

body:
Instead of removing SSE completely, we should add WebSockets as an alternative, that can be enabled via a config flag. 
Once we are ready to release v1 of n8n, we could then make WebSockets the default option, based on user feedback. 

[I've updated this branch with the latest code, and updated the push code to support either SSE or WebSockets.](https://github.com/n8n-io/n8n/compare/master...ws-sse-push?expand=1)

@matthijs166 Since I'm unable to push to your branch, can you please cherry pick the last 2 commits from the branch I've linked above?
Once that's done, we can get the review process started.

For anyone wanting to test these changes, a custom docker image is available now `n8nio/n8n:ws-sse-push`
author: netroy
netroy

body: Uh wow. Thanks for catching this. But should that not also just happen if we use the not default mode of storing binary data? Because for most people that query should not be required at all.
author: janober

body:
> Uh wow. Thanks for catching this. But should that not also just happen if we use the not default mode of storing binary data? Because for most people that query should not be required at all.

This affects only instances where `EXECUTIONS_DATA_PRUNE` is set to `true`. This includes all our cloud customers.
author: netroy

body:
Ah yes, only happens if prune is activated.

But I meant the query does currently only have to run if EXECUTIONS_DATA_PRUNE === true && BINARY_MODE === "filesystem".

For everybody that does not use filesystem mode the query runs and then the data does get discarded again unused.
author: janober

body: Or to say it differently. It currently does that query that the binary files on the filesystem get deleted. But for not filesystem mode there are not files to be deleted as the binary data is saved in the database together with the execution.
author: janober

body: @janober you are right. pushed another commit to skip the query in default mode.

author: CLAassistant

body:
@psxvoid 
Hey Pavel, thanks a lot for this enhancement to the Nextcloud node—this is exactly the feature I needed! Previously, I had to use a workaround with curl, so this will be a big improvement.

I've patched this PR into my n8n version 1.95.3 for testing. It's working as expected, but I did notice it changes the node’s default behavior: if you don’t configure any `include fields`, the node throws an error (`Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'length')`). You need to manually press “Add Field” to restore the default behavior. That’s a breaking change for existing workflows using the Nextcloud node, since it would require users to update their nodes.

For now, I’ll keep using the patched version, but I’d suggest updating the implementation so it remains backwards-compatible and doesn’t force users to update every Nextcloud node in their workflows.

Thanks again for your work on this!
author: semidark

body:
hey @psxvoid ,

me again. After giving it a second thought i fixed the default behavior of the patched NextCloud node to be identical to the original NextCloud node adn added the missing fields that are mentioned [in the officia NextCloud Documentation](https://docs.nextcloud.com/server/latest/developer_manual/client_apis/WebDAV/basic.html)

See... https://github.com/semidark/n8n/tree/enhance-nextcloud-node

author: colzzky

body:
Yes, if it is only internally, then it is 100% OK as you mentioned.

But be careful. Looks like you did submit some keys to GitHub, which should not be public. Additionally, be also aware that keys like that should never end up in the source code (even if it would not be public). What you are doing with OAuth should rather be done with credential overwrites. You can find the docs here:
https://docs.n8n.io/embed/configuration/

body:
Great PR! Please pay attention to the following items before merging:

__Files matching `packages/**`:__
- [ ] If fixing bug, added test to cover scenario.
- [ ] If addressing forum or Github issue, added link to description.

__Files matching `packages/**/*.ts`:__
- [ ] Added unit tests to cover new or updated functionality.

__Files matching `packages/cli/src/databases/migrations/**`:__
- [ ] Requested review from at least two engineers on migration.
- [ ] Avoided irreversible data migrations.
- [ ] Avoided deleting or updating data keys.
- [ ] Wrote 'down' migration if possible.

__Files matching `packages/nodes-base/nodes/**`:__
- [ ] Added workflow tests for nodes if possible.

__Files matching `packages/nodes-base/package.json`:__
- [ ] Avoided adding dependencies for nodes if not absolutely necessary.


Make sure to check off this list before asking for review.

body:
Somehow this PR skipped my attention. 
I have a [PR](https://github.com/n8n-io/js-client-oauth2-extended/pull/1) on our client-oauth fork to convert the code to TS, and to remove the bits that we never use. 
Should we try to port over that code into this repo. 
That way we can switch to using the same http request mechanisms for oauth2 that we use for the rest of the code. That would enable us to do things like allowing self-signed certificates.

body:
@cstuncsik can we perhaps avoid querying for `nodes` and `connections` altogether? I'd recommend doing this using an optional third argument (I'd suggest an object called `options` so we can extend it in the future).

I know from Adi's comment that the `nodes` are used in the `workflows.controller.ee.ts` file to populate a key called `usedCredentials`.

The purpose of `usedCredentials` was to allow sharing from the workflows list to be possible. Now that we fetch the sharing data when the user clicks the `Share` button, we don't need this information anymore.

Can you please check if the frontend uses this `usedCredentials` attribute to something else? If not, we can remove this one, and then prevent querying for `nodes` and `connections` that are both heavy and unnecessary here.

If it's not clear, please let me know and we can chat about it.
author: krynble

body: @krynble now looking at it maybe my first solution was ok. The `nodes` property is never used where workflows queried by the `getMany` function so I think we can remove the `nodes` field from `getMany`. Where the `nodes` needed

body: this seems like a really simple change - how can we progress this?
author: RTJoe

body:
For those running n8n with Redis Sentinel (not directly supported yet), an alternative is to put HAProxy in TCP mode in front of your Redis nodes.
Configure it with a TCP health check that runs:
	1.	PING → expect +PONG
	2.	INFO REPLICATION → expect role:master

Only the current master is marked UP, so HAProxy automatically redirects connections when Sentinel promotes a new master.
Your n8n then connects to a single stable host:port (the HAProxy endpoint), without needing native Sentinel support.

Performance impact is negligible for most workloads, since HAProxy forwards traffic at L4 with minimal latency.
author: josimarjmv

author: janober

body:
Hi @janober - I'm new to n8n and loving it. Like I said, ideally OAuth2 w/ PKCE would be implemented but this seems (to me*) as a good stop gap.

I was also looking at adding a few more operations/actions like some of the new available-to-everyone (previously Enterprise plan only) metadata APIs to [list the bases a token has access to](https://airtable.com/developers/web/api/list-bases) as well as [the schema of a given base](https://airtable.com/developers/web/api/get-base-schema). Those changes would touch more of the actual node code. Would you generally prefer separate PRs?

*Just a little disclaimer - while I work at Airtable, I am not contributing officially; I'm using n8n for some side projects :)
author: marks

body:
Thanks a lot! And great to hear that you love n8n! 🙏 

Yes separate PRs would be a great idea. 

Regarding PKCE, that is something which should be added soon ish.

Thanks a lot for your help/contribution!
author: janober

body:
> Regarding PKCE, that is something which should be added soon ish.

Awesome. When that is published, you might consider trying to deprecate PATs/legacy user API keys in n8n or strongly encourage people to use OAuth somehow. It seems typeform in n8n also has the two auth options 🙌 

From my POV, this should be good to merge but please do your own testing. I will reference this PR in any follow ups as some of those new endpoints will not work for most people without using a PAT (or OAuth :)) in the header (which this PR implements)

Cheers!
author: marks

body: Great, sounds good. Thanks a lot!

Great PR! Please pay attention to the following items before merging:

- [ ] Spoofed endpoints that are not critical for the test (to avoid flakiness).
- [ ] Picked most efficient path to start the test (for example skipped account setup and starting at /workflow/new for a canvas test).
- [ ] Avoided adding waits on time (use request intercepts instead).
- [ ] Ensured each spec does not depend on any another spec to pass.


Make sure to check off this list before asking for review.

how to use this?

author: joshxyzhimself

body:
@joshxyzhimself This PR added support for websockets as a push mechanism for internal api calls in n8n. 
This does not add a WebSocket node. [There is a proof-of-concept PR](https://github.com/n8n-io/n8n/pull/3393), but we currently do not have an ETA on that.
author: netroy

body:
Got it.

I somehow wish uws (https://github.com/uNetworking/uWebSockets.js) gets used over ws though, which has built-in pub-sub feature.

Possible triggers:

- websocket on connect
- websocket on message
- websocket on disconnect

Possible actions:

- websocket unicast message
- websocket multicast message
- websocket broadcast message
- websocket subscribe
- websocket unsubscribe
- websocket close connection
author: joshxyzhimself

body:
@joshxyzhimself We originally wanted to use uWebSockets.js, but it's not available on NPM, and we don't want to deal with the hassle of switching registries or pulling packages from multiple places right now.
We also had some issues with using uws on alpine linux (in our docker image), and I think adding `libc` didn't help either (but, I'm not certain about that).

`ws` satisfies all our current requirements. 
Perhaps in the future if our requirements change, we'll reconsider this decision then.

author: janober

body:
Hey @chrtkv,

Thanks for the PR, At the moment we have a User > Get option where you can get a user based on their username or ID, Would it not be better to have `By Email` as an option for that so it would fit in this list...

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/4688521/219055750-cad40f6f-506b-47c6-a625-210c469cdccc.png)

Or is there a better reason for having as it's own option?
author: Joffcom

body:
Hey @Joffcom, sorry for the slow response! Thanks for checking out my pull request.

I agree, it makes sense, and I even started rewriting the code, but I realized that it would be better to leave 'find' as a separate option.  I think 'get' and 'find' serve slightly different purposes in this case. 'Get' assumes the user exists and we need info about them, but with 'find', the user might not exist or might not be found. So, they work with different things - 'get' with IDs and 'find' with emails - and they also have different API endpoints. If we decide to implement the search inside 'get', the code will become more complex, I think
author: chrtkv

body:
Hey there,

Hope this message finds you well. I just wanted to follow up on the status of this pull request 🙂
author: chrtkv

body:
Hey @chrtkv,

I have not yet done anything with it, I still need to create an internal ticket for it as well which I will get done when I am back on Monday. I do very much like the idea and I can see how it would be useful, Just need to have a play with it and think about the find vs search thing.
author: Joffcom

body: Bump, this is an important feature for integrating nodes things that have different user account IDs, the only common data to identify a user is usually the email address

body: Let me repeat my proposal from a few weeks ago: Migrations affecting the potentially gigantic executions_entity table are generally very problematic because of the sheer volume of that table. Therefore, we should consider splitting that table in two: In one we only store the execution data blob, in the other all the other (lightweight) columns. JOINing them when needed is still straightforward and future migrations should no longer be problematic. Thoughts?
author: csuermann

body:
I like the idea @csuermann , this might greatly speed things up.
I'll try creating a PR about this and see how this goes.
author: krynble

body:
Looks like `typedi` has issues with circular dependencies.
Since the `PurgeInvalidWorkflowConnections` migration, `NodeTypes` and `LoadNodesAndCredentials` depend on each other. as a short term fix I've changed some imports to be dynamic, but in the long run we need to get rid of circular dependencies like these.
author: netroy

body:
> Do you have plans to migrate other singletons as well, such as the LoggerProxy?

Yeah. There is already a part 2 in progress, where all singletons will using this pattern, and we'll also have automatic constructor injection wherever possible.

body:
Hi @pigri,

Thanks for the contribution, We have now created a Community Node Repository which is our preferred method for new node submissions.

Once a node has been published to the Community Node Repository users will be able to install the node from their n8n interface and will be able to use your node quicker, You will also be able to update the node as needed without needing to wait for the review process.

In the future we may reach out to you about including your node in our main release, But for now I am going to close this PR.

You can find more information in the links below, If you have any questions about this or need any help let us know.

**Building Community Nodes:** https://docs.n8n.io/integrations/community-nodes/build-community-nodes/
**Submitting Community Nodes:** https://docs.n8n.io/integrations/creating-nodes/deploy/submit-community-nodes/
**Published Nodes:** https://www.npmjs.com/search?q=keywords:n8n-community-node-package
